framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Enhancing the quality, consistency, and transparency of the capital base, along with addressing systemic risk.
policies.scope: Global banking systems
policies.objective: To create a more resilient banking system through improved capital requirements.

policies.scope: Applicable banking entities
policies.objective: To clarify which banks and financial institutions are subject to the regulations.

policies.policy_title: Basel III: A global regulatory framework for more resilient banks and banking systems
policies.policy_description: This document presents the Basel Committee’s reforms to strengthen global capital and liquidity rules with the goal of promoting a more resilient banking sector.
policies.scope: Global banking sector
policies.objective: To improve the banking sector’s ability to absorb shocks arising from financial and economic stress.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The reforms raise both the quality and quantity of the regulatory capital base and enhance the risk coverage of the capital framework.
subpolicies.control: Includes a leverage ratio as a backstop to risk-based capital measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Strengthens bank-level regulation to raise the resilience of individual banking institutions.
subpolicies.control: Focuses on individual bank stability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Addresses system-wide risks that can build up across the banking sector.
subpolicies.control: Aims to mitigate procyclical amplification of risks.

policies.policy_description: Ensures that banks' risk exposures are backed by a high-quality capital base and improves the consistency and transparency of capital definitions across jurisdictions.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To enhance the quality, consistency, and transparency of the capital base to mitigate systemic risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mandates that the predominant form of Tier 1 capital must be common shares and retained earnings.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with international standards for Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishes harmonized deductions from capital and prudential filters applied at the level of common equity or its equivalent.
subpolicies.control: Regular audits to ensure compliance with harmonized capital deductions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies that the remainder of the Tier 1 capital base must consist of subordinated instruments with specific characteristics.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of Tier 1 capital composition for compliance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Phasing Out Innovative Hybrid Capital Instruments
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Phases out innovative hybrid capital instruments with redemption incentives, currently limited to 15% of the Tier 1 capital base.
subpolicies.control: Implementation timeline and monitoring of hybrid capital instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Improves market discipline by enhancing the transparency of the capital base, requiring detailed disclosures.
subpolicies.control: Mandatory reporting and reconciliation of capital elements to reported accounts.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing counterparty credit risk in banks using models.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks utilizing models for counterparty credit risk management.
policies.objective: To ensure banks have sound and integrated counterparty credit risk management systems.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must conduct regular backtesting to compare risk measures generated by models against realized risk measures.
subpolicies.control: Regular program of backtesting must be established.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Initial validation and ongoing periodic review of the IMM model must be independent of the model developers.
subpolicies.control: Independent validation and review processes must be documented.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The board and senior management must be actively involved in the risk control process.
subpolicies.control: Daily reports must be reviewed by senior management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The internal risk measurement exposure model must be integrated into daily risk management processes.
subpolicies.control: Outputs of the risk measurement model must be used in planning and monitoring.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks should have a routine to ensure compliance with internal policies regarding the risk measurement system.
subpolicies.control: Documentation of the risk management system must be maintained.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regular independent reviews of the risk measurement system must be conducted.
subpolicies.control: Reviews should address adequacy of documentation and integration of risk measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must document the process for initial and ongoing validation of their IMM model.
subpolicies.control: Documentation must allow third parties to recreate analysis.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must define criteria to assess their EPE models and document the process for performance assessment.
subpolicies.control: Written policy must describe remediation for unacceptable performance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must define how representative counterparty portfolios are constructed for validating EPE models.
subpolicies.control: Criteria for portfolio construction must be documented.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Validation must assess more than a single statistic of the model distribution.
subpolicies.control: Validation processes must include comprehensive statistical assessments.

policies.policy_description: Banks must validate their EPE models and relevant risk measures to ensure accuracy and compliance with regulatory standards.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks using EPE models for counterparty credit risk management.
policies.objective: To ensure the reliability and effectiveness of EPE models in measuring counterparty credit risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must conduct static, historical backtesting on representative counterparty portfolios at regular intervals as directed by supervisors.
subpolicies.control: Backtesting must be based on sensitivity to material risk factors and correlations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The performance of EPE models must be subject to good backtesting practices capable of identifying poor performance.
subpolicies.control: Regular assessments of model performance across all counterparties.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: An independent risk control unit must be responsible for the design and implementation of the counterparty credit risk management system.
subpolicies.control: The unit must produce daily reports on risk measurement model outputs and evaluate relationships between exposure measures and trading limits.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks may use a more conservative measure than the regulatory EAD metric with prior supervisory approval, which must be validated regularly.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of relative conservatism during initial and regular supervisory reviews.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Pricing models used to calculate counterparty exposure must be regularly tested against independent benchmarks.
subpolicies.control: Ongoing model validation process including recent performance assessments.

policies.policy_description: Addressing reliance on external credit ratings and minimizing cliff effects in risk assessment.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks investing in debt instruments.
policies.objective: To ensure appropriate risk weights are applied based on credit assessments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Modification of risk weight application based on specific assessments of issued debt.
subpolicies.control: High quality credit assessments can only be applied if the bank's claim ranks pari passu or senior to the assessed claim.

policies.policy_description: A global regulatory framework aimed at enhancing the resilience of banks and banking systems.
policies.scope: Global banking institutions
policies.objective: To establish a more resilient banking system through improved capital standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Integration of Basel III standards into the consolidated Basel Framework.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with Basel III standards as outlined by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision.

sections.title: Raising the quality, consistency and transparency of the capital base

policies.policy_title: Raising the quality, consistency and transparency of the capital base
policies.policy_description: This policy aims to enhance the quality, consistency, and transparency of the capital base in banking institutions.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks and banking systems under the Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks maintain a robust capital base that can withstand financial stress and promote stability in the banking system.

sections.title: Supplementing the risk-based capital requirement with a leverage ratio

policies.policy_title: Supplementing the risk-based capital requirement with a leverage ratio
policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the approach to supplement the risk-based capital requirements with a leverage ratio to ensure financial stability.
policies.scope: Applicable to financial institutions under the jurisdiction of the Bank for International Settlements.
policies.objective: To enhance the resilience of the banking sector by providing a backstop to the risk-based capital ratios.

sections.title: Reducing procyclicality and promoting countercyclical buffers

policies.policy_title: Reducing procyclicality and promoting countercyclical buffers
policies.policy_description: This policy aims to mitigate the effects of economic cycles on financial stability by implementing countercyclical buffers.
policies.scope: Applicable to financial institutions and regulatory bodies
policies.objective: To enhance the resilience of the financial system during economic downturns and prevent excessive risk-taking during booms.

policies.policy_description: Provisioning that anticipates future needs and requirements.
policies.scope: Applicable to all provisioning activities within the organization.
policies.objective: To ensure that provisioning is aligned with future demands and operational needs.

policies.policy_description: Policy aimed at preserving capital within the organization.
policies.scope: Applicable to all financial operations and investments.
policies.objective: To ensure that the capital is maintained and not eroded over time.

policies.policy_description: Framework addressing excess credit growth in banking systems.
policies.scope: Global banking institutions
policies.objective: To ensure resilience in banks and banking systems through effective credit growth management.

policies.policy_description: A global regulatory framework aimed at strengthening capital and liquidity rules for banks.
policies.scope: Global banking sector
policies.objective: To promote a more resilient banking sector capable of absorbing financial shocks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A requirement for banks to hold sufficient liquid assets to cover short-term obligations.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring and reporting of liquidity positions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A standard requiring banks to maintain a stable funding profile in relation to their assets and off-balance sheet activities.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of funding stability over a one-year horizon.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Enhancements to risk management practices and governance structures in banks.
subpolicies.control: Regular audits and compliance checks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for banks to improve their transparency and disclosures to stakeholders.
subpolicies.control: Periodic reporting and public disclosures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Efforts to strengthen the resolution framework for banks that are deemed systemically important.
subpolicies.control: Cross-border resolution planning and coordination.

policies.policy_description: A global regulatory framework aimed at strengthening capital and liquidity rules for banks to promote resilience in the banking sector.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and banking systems globally.
policies.objective: To improve the banking sector's ability to absorb shocks from financial and economic stress and reduce spillover risks to the real economy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Enhancements to risk management practices and governance structures within banks.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of comprehensive risk management frameworks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Strengthening banks' transparency and disclosure requirements to improve market confidence.
subpolicies.control: Regular reporting and disclosure of financial health and risk exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recommendations to enhance national resolution powers and their cross-border implementation.
subpolicies.control: Establishment of effective cross-border resolution frameworks.

policies.scope: Global banking sector

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing credit protection and capital conservation for banks.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using the Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure resilience in banking systems through effective management of credit exposures and capital buffers.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognizing credit protection provided to securitisation exposures.
subpolicies.control: Entities must be rated BBB- or better, and meet specific requirements outlined in paragraphs 189 to 194.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for banks on the use of solicited and unsolicited ratings from eligible credit assessment institutions.
subpolicies.control: Banks must use chosen ECAIs consistently and may only use unsolicited ratings if they meet quality standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulations governing the operation of capital conservation buffers for banks.
subpolicies.control: Banks must maintain capital buffers above regulatory minimums and manage discretionary distributions to rebuild buffers.

policies.policy_description: The Basel Committee is raising the resilience of the banking sector by strengthening the regulatory capital framework, building on the three pillars of the Basel II framework.
policies.scope: Global banking sector
policies.objective: To enhance the quality and quantity of the regulatory capital base and address systemic risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Raising the quality, consistency and transparency of the capital base
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensures that banks’ risk exposures are backed by a high quality capital base, with a predominant form of Tier 1 capital being common shares and retained earnings.
subpolicies.control: Harmonisation of deductions from capital and prudential filters, and improvement of market discipline through enhanced transparency.

sections.title: Part 1: Minimum capital requirements and buffers

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks to enhance resilience and risk coverage.
policies.scope: Applicable to internationally active banks
policies.objective: To strengthen the capital framework and mitigate systemic risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implements reforms to capture major on- and off-balance sheet risks and enhance capital requirements for trading book and securitisation exposures.
subpolicies.control: Pillar 1 and Pillar 3 enhancements must be implemented by the end of 2011.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Strengthens capital requirements for counterparty credit exposures arising from derivatives, repo, and securities financing activities.
subpolicies.control: Banks must determine capital requirements using stressed inputs and apply capital charges for CVA risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduces a leverage ratio to constrain excessive leverage in the banking sector and mitigate destabilising deleveraging processes.
subpolicies.control: Leverage ratio calculated comparably across jurisdictions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Reducing Procyclicality and Promoting Countercyclical Buffers
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Addresses procyclical amplification of financial shocks and promotes countercyclical buffers.
subpolicies.control: Measures to mitigate reliance on external ratings and enhance internal assessments.

policies.policy_description: Measures to ensure banks are resilient to procyclical dynamics and serve as shock absorbers in the financial system.
policies.scope: Banking Sector
policies.objective: To dampen excess cyclicality of minimum capital requirements and promote forward-looking provisions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Safeguards to address excess cyclicality in minimum capital requirements, including long-term data for default probabilities and stress testing.
subpolicies.control: Use of long-term data horizons, downturn loss-given-default estimates, and stress tests.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promoting stronger provisioning practices through an expected loss approach and updating supervisory guidance.
subpolicies.control: Support for IASB's expected loss approach and updating supervisory guidance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Framework to promote conservation of capital and build adequate buffers for stress periods.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of internationally agreed capital conservation standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjusting capital buffer ranges when signs of excessive credit growth are detected.
subpolicies.control: Introduction of a countercyclical buffer to protect the banking sector.

policies.policy_description: Establishes capital measures to address unexpected losses and cyclicality, requiring banks to maintain strong capital buffers above minimum requirements.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banking institutions
policies.objective: To ensure banking sector stability and resilience against severe shocks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires systemically important banks to have loss absorbing capacity beyond minimum standards to address systemic risks.
subpolicies.control: Development of methodologies to assess systemic importance and additional loss absorbency requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduces internationally harmonized liquidity standards to ensure banks maintain a strong liquidity base.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of the Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) and Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR) as minimum standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promotes resilience to liquidity disruptions over a thirty-day horizon by ensuring sufficient high-quality liquid assets.
subpolicies.control: Requirements for unencumbered, liquid assets to offset net cash outflows during acute stress scenarios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires banks to maintain a minimum amount of stable funding relative to the liquidity profiles of their assets.
subpolicies.control: Incentives for banks to fund activities with stable sources of funding on an ongoing basis.

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum capital requirements and buffers for banking organizations to ensure financial stability.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banking organizations under Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks maintain sufficient capital to absorb losses and support lending during economic downturns.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires banks to maintain a minimum level of liquid assets to cover short-term liquidity needs.
subpolicies.control: LCR must be at least 100% at all times.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Aims to limit reliance on short-term wholesale funding and promote stable funding over a one-year horizon.
subpolicies.control: NSFR must be implemented as a minimum standard by 1 January 2018.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishes a set of metrics for supervisors to monitor liquidity risk profiles of banks.
subpolicies.control: Metrics include contractual maturity mismatch, concentration of funding, available unencumbered assets, and LCR by currency.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the transitional arrangements for implementing new capital standards to support lending while meeting higher capital requirements.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of LCR and NSFR will be monitored with rigorous reporting processes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the components of capital required for banks, focusing on common equity as the highest quality capital.
subpolicies.control: Common Equity Tier 1 must be at least 4.5% of risk-weighted assets.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the criteria for the calculation of Common Equity Tier 1 capital and Additional Tier 1 capital for banks.
policies.scope: Applicable to internationally active banks and their capital instruments.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks maintain adequate capital to absorb losses and support financial stability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Common Equity Tier 1 Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the criteria that instruments must meet to be classified as Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must represent the most subordinated claim, be perpetual, and not create expectations of redemption.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the minimum criteria for instruments to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be issued and paid-in, subordinated, perpetual, and callable only under specific conditions.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks to ensure financial stability.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banking organizations under the Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks maintain sufficient capital to absorb losses and support ongoing operations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Dividends and coupons must be paid out of distributable items and cannot have credit sensitive features.
subpolicies.control: Prohibits 'dividend pushers' and requires full discretion to cancel distributions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments classified as liabilities must have principal loss absorption mechanisms.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must either convert to common shares or have a write-down mechanism.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Neither the bank nor related parties can purchase the instrument or fund its purchase.
subpolicies.control: Ensures independence of capital instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot have features that hinder recapitalization.
subpolicies.control: Prohibits compensation provisions for investors if new instruments are issued at lower prices.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the criteria for instruments to be included in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be subordinated, have a minimum maturity, and not have credit sensitive features.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provisions held against future losses qualify for inclusion in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Provisions must be freely available to meet losses that materialize.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks to ensure financial stability.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks under the Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain sufficient capital to absorb losses and support ongoing operations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limits the inclusion of general provisions and loan-loss reserves in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Maximum of 1.25 percentage points of credit risk-weighted risk assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognizing minority interests in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Must meet criteria for classification as common shares and the subsidiary must be a bank.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which Tier 1 capital instruments from subsidiaries can be recognized.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must meet criteria for classification as Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for recognizing total capital instruments from fully consolidated subsidiaries.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must meet criteria for classification as Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Capital Issued through Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs)
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions for including capital issued through SPVs in Additional Tier 1 or Tier 2.
subpolicies.control: Must meet relevant entry criteria and the SPV's only asset must be its investment in the bank's capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines regulatory adjustments to be applied to regulatory capital calculations.
subpolicies.control: Goodwill and all other intangibles must be deducted from Common Equity Tier 1.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks to ensure financial stability.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks under Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital levels to absorb losses and support ongoing operations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: DTAs that rely on future profitability must be deducted from Common Equity Tier 1. DTAs can be netted with DTLs only if they relate to the same taxation authority.
subpolicies.control: Ensure compliance with local GAAP and IFRS definitions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The cash flow hedge reserve related to non-fair valued items must be derecognised in Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.
subpolicies.control: Identify and adjust for elements causing artificial volatility.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Shortfall of Provisions to Expected Losses
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Full deduction from capital for any shortfall in provisions compared to expected losses under the IRB approach.
subpolicies.control: Maintain adequate provisions to meet expected loss requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Gain on Sale Related to Securitisation Transactions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Any increase in equity capital from securitisation transactions must be derecognised in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Monitor securitisation transactions for compliance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Defined Benefit Pension Fund Assets and Liabilities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defined benefit pension liabilities must be fully recognised in Common Equity Tier 1, and assets deducted unless offsetting is approved.
subpolicies.control: Assess the accessibility of pension fund assets for withdrawal.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All investments in own common shares must be deducted from Common Equity Tier 1, including contractual obligations to purchase.
subpolicies.control: Prevent double counting of capital through careful accounting.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reciprocal cross holdings designed to inflate capital positions must be deducted in full using a corresponding deduction approach.
subpolicies.control: Apply deductions consistently across capital components.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory adjustments for investments in capital of banking, financial, and insurance entities outside the scope of regulatory consolidation.
policies.scope: Investments in entities where the bank does not own more than 10% of issued common share capital.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital buffers against investments in other financial entities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Includes direct, indirect, and synthetic holdings of capital instruments, with specific conditions for underwriting positions.
subpolicies.control: Holdings in both banking and trading books are included; underwriting positions held for five working days or less can be excluded.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If total holdings exceed 10% of the bank's common equity, the excess must be deducted from the corresponding capital tier.
subpolicies.control: Deduction applies to the same component of capital for which the capital would qualify if issued by the bank.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If a bank lacks sufficient capital in a particular tier to satisfy a deduction, the shortfall is deducted from the next higher tier.
subpolicies.control: Shortfalls in Additional Tier 1 capital are deducted from Common Equity Tier 1.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Amounts below the deduction threshold will continue to be risk-weighted according to applicable rules.
subpolicies.control: Instruments in the trading book treated per market risk rules; banking book instruments treated per internal ratings-based or standardized approach.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulatory adjustment applies to investments in entities where the bank owns more than 10% of issued common share capital or where the entity is an affiliate.
subpolicies.control: Investments must be fully deducted following a corresponding deduction approach.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks to ensure financial stability.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks under the Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital to absorb losses and support lending.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Items that may receive limited recognition when calculating Common Equity Tier 1, capped at 10% of the bank’s common equity.
subpolicies.control: Includes significant investments in common shares of unconsolidated financial institutions, mortgage servicing rights, and deferred tax assets from temporary differences.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for banks to disclose regulatory capital elements and adjustments to improve transparency.
subpolicies.control: Full reconciliation of regulatory capital elements, separate disclosure of regulatory adjustments, and description of capital instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Phased implementation of new capital standards to ensure banks can meet higher capital requirements.
subpolicies.control: Includes specific timelines for minimum capital requirements and adjustments to Common Equity Tier 1.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the requirements for capital instruments and their treatment based on redemption incentives and effective maturity dates.
policies.scope: Applicable to capital instruments recognized in Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital.
policies.objective: To ensure that capital instruments meet the new criteria for inclusion in regulatory capital and to manage the transition of instruments based on their characteristics.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Treatment of Instruments with Redemption Incentives Prior to 2013
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments with a call and step-up prior to 1 January 2013 will be recognized in Tier 1 or Tier 2 if they meet new criteria at maturity.
subpolicies.control: Instruments not called at maturity will be assessed for compliance with new criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Treatment of Instruments with Redemption Incentives On or After 2013
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments with a call and step-up on or after 1 January 2013 will be recognized in Tier 1 or Tier 2 if they meet new criteria at maturity.
subpolicies.control: Instruments not called at maturity will be phased out if they do not meet new criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Derecognition of Instruments Not Meeting New Criteria
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments that do not meet the new criteria for inclusion in Tier 1 or Tier 2 will be fully derecognized from 1 January 2013.
subpolicies.control: Instruments will be assessed for compliance with new criteria prior to effective maturity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments that no longer qualify as Additional Tier 1 or Tier 2 will be phased out starting 1 January 2013.
subpolicies.control: Instruments will be monitored for compliance with the new criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Capital instruments that do not meet the criteria for inclusion in Common Equity Tier 1 will be excluded as of 1 January 2013.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be evaluated against the specified conditions for inclusion.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Enhancements to the counterparty credit risk framework to ensure all material risks are captured.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of a revised metric for counterparty credit risk effective 1 January 2013.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Effective EPE Calculation for IMM Banks
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must use the greater of portfolio-level capital charge based on Effective EPE using current market data and stress calibration.
subpolicies.control: Regular assessment of stress calibration and model parameters.

policies.policy_description: A bank must add a capital charge to cover the risk of mark-to-market losses on expected counterparty risk (CVA) to OTC derivatives.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks with counterparty credit risk.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital to cover potential CVA losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Advanced CVA Risk Capital Charge for Banks with IMM Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks with IMM approval must calculate the CVA capital charge by modeling the impact of changes in counterparties’ credit spreads on CVAs of all OTC derivative counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Calculation must adhere to specified formulas and methodologies.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The formula for CVA must be based on specific inputs, including credit spreads, expected exposure, and loss given default.
subpolicies.control: Inputs must reflect market assessments and be based on approved VaR models.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Non-IMM netting sets must be included in the CVA risk capital charge by assuming a constant expected exposure profile.
subpolicies.control: Approach must be consistent with the maximum maturity of transactions in the netting set.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: When CDS spreads are not available, banks must use appropriate proxy spreads based on rating, industry, and region.
subpolicies.control: Proxy spreads must be justified and documented.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks regarding CVA risk charges.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks calculating CVA risk capital charges.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital to cover CVA risks associated with exposures to counterparties.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the conditions under which banks can use the advanced CVA risk charge and the requirements for its calculation.
subpolicies.control: Only exposures with supervisory approval for specific risk modeling are included.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the formula and requirements for banks that do not have approvals to use the advanced CVA risk charge.
subpolicies.control: Calculation must include weights based on external ratings and consider collateral effects.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies the types of hedges that can be included in the CVA risk capital charge calculation.
subpolicies.control: Only single-name CDSs, single-name contingent CDSs, and index CDSs are eligible; other hedges are treated as separate instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Calculation of Aggregate CCR and CVA Risk Capital Charges
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Describes the aggregation process for default risk capital charges and CVA risk capital charges.
subpolicies.control: Outstanding EAD is net of incurred CVA losses.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the capital charge requirements for banks based on different approaches to counterparty credit risk.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks with different levels of IMM approval and market-risk internal-models approval.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital buffers to cover counterparty credit risk exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Banks with IMM Approval and Market-Risk Internal-Models Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determines total CCR capital charge as the sum of the higher IMM capital charge based on current and stressed parameter calibrations, and the advanced CVA risk capital charge.
subpolicies.control: Requires demonstration of specific VaR model effects on rating migrations to national supervisor.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Banks with IMM Approval and Without Specific Risk VaR Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determines total CCR capital charge as the sum of the higher IMM capital charge based on current and stressed parameter calibrations, and the standardized CVA risk capital charge.
subpolicies.control: Standardized CVA risk capital charge determined by specified paragraph.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determines total CCR capital charge as the sum of CEM or SM based capital charge and standardized CVA risk capital charge.
subpolicies.control: EADs determined by specified paragraphs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must identify exposures that give rise to general wrong-way risk and conduct stress testing and scenario analyses.
subpolicies.control: Regular reporting to senior management on wrong-way risks and management steps.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Specific Wrong-Way Risk Identification and Treatment
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires banks to have policies for identifying and treating exposures with specific wrong-way risk differently in EAD calculations.
subpolicies.control: Procedures must be in place from trade inception through its life.

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks under various risk scenarios.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using Advanced or Foundation IRB and Standardised Approach.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital to cover potential losses from various exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Loss Given Default (LGD) for Swap Transactions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Sets LGD to 100% for Advanced or Foundation IRB banks on swap transactions.
subpolicies.control: Ensure capital requirements for underlying exposures are calculated without reduction for swaps.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Asset Value Correlation Multiplier for Large Financial Institutions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implements a multiplier of 1.25 for correlation parameters of exposures to financial institutions with total assets >= $100 billion.
subpolicies.control: Use the most recent audited financial statement to determine asset size.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Margin Period of Risk for Collateralised Counterparties
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Increases the margin period of risk for transactions subject to daily re-margining and mark-to-market valuation.
subpolicies.control: Impose supervisory floors based on the nature of netting sets and historical margin call disputes.

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum holding periods for various transaction types and outlines requirements for internal market risk models.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To ensure adequate capital buffers and manage market risk effectively.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines minimum holding periods for different transaction types including repo-style transactions, capital market transactions, and secured lending.
subpolicies.control: Minimum holding periods are set at five business days for repo-style transactions, ten business days for other capital market transactions, and twenty business days for secured lending.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Sets criteria for the recognition of internal market risk models for repo-style and similar transactions.
subpolicies.control: Quantitative and qualitative criteria must align with Basel II guidelines, with adjustments for holding periods based on transaction type.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Describes the shortcut method for estimating Effective Expected Positive Exposure (EPE) for margined counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Effective EPE must be calculated considering collateral agreements and potential exposure changes during the margin period of risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Prohibits the capture of EAD reductions due to downgrade triggers in collateral agreements.
subpolicies.control: New paragraph 41(iv) mandates that banks using internal models must not account for EAD reductions from collateral clauses related to credit quality deterioration.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines operational requirements for the collateral management unit within banks.
subpolicies.control: The unit must manage margin calls, disputes, and maintain accurate reporting on collateral, with sufficient resources allocated by senior management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mandates regular internal reporting on collateral management to senior management.
subpolicies.control: Reports must include details on types of collateral, margin call disputes, and trends in collateral management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires regular independent reviews of the Counterparty Credit Risk (CCR) management system.
subpolicies.control: Reviews must assess documentation adequacy, collateral management unit organization, and overall CCR management processes at least annually.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks to manage counterparty credit risks effectively.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks employing internal models for risk management.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital and manage risks associated with counterparty credit effectively.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Integration of counterparty credit risk measures into daily risk management practices.
subpolicies.control: Ensure daily monitoring and management of CCR measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Approval Process for Risk Pricing Models
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Approval process for risk pricing models and valuation systems used by front and back-office personnel.
subpolicies.control: Establish a formal approval process for risk models.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Validation of CCR Measurement Process Changes
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Validation of any significant changes in the counterparty credit risk measurement process.
subpolicies.control: Implement validation procedures for changes in measurement processes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Define the scope of counterparty credit risks captured by the risk measurement model.
subpolicies.control: Ensure comprehensive coverage of counterparty credit risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensure the integrity of the management information system used for CCR data.
subpolicies.control: Regular audits and checks on the management information system.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensure the accuracy and completeness of counterparty credit risk data.
subpolicies.control: Implement data validation and reconciliation processes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Legal Terms Reflection in Exposure Measurements
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Accurate reflection of legal terms in collateral and netting agreements into exposure measurements.
subpolicies.control: Review and update exposure measurement processes to reflect legal agreements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Verification of the consistency, timeliness, and reliability of data sources used in internal models.
subpolicies.control: Establish independence and reliability checks for data sources.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Accuracy and appropriateness of volatility and correlation assumptions used in models.
subpolicies.control: Regular review and validation of assumptions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensure accuracy of valuation and risk transformation calculations.
subpolicies.control: Implement checks and balances for valuation calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Verification of the model’s accuracy through frequent backtesting.
subpolicies.control: Conduct regular backtesting of risk models.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Cash Management Policies for Collateral Reuse
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements on controls regarding the reuse of collateral by IMM banks.
subpolicies.control: Ensure cash management policies account for liquidity risks and collateral reuse.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Supervisory Haircuts for Non-Cash OTC Collateral
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implementation of supervisory haircuts for non-cash OTC collateral.
subpolicies.control: Use haircuts that meet financial collateral standards in EAD calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement for banks to model non-cash collateral jointly with underlying securities.
subpolicies.control: Ensure joint modeling of collateral in EAD calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Revision of credit risk mitigation section to add a qualitative collateral management requirement.
subpolicies.control: Devote sufficient resources to the operation of margin agreements.

policies.policy_description: A global regulatory framework for more resilient banks and banking systems.
policies.scope: Banks and banking systems
policies.objective: To enhance the resilience of banks by strengthening capital requirements and risk coverage.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Strengthening the risk coverage of the capital framework to capture major on- and off-balance sheet risks.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of reforms to raise capital requirements for trading books and complex securitisation exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Strengthening capital requirements for counterparty credit exposures arising from banks’ derivatives and securities financing activities.
subpolicies.control: Banks must determine capital requirements using stressed inputs and apply capital charges for potential mark-to-market losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introducing a leverage ratio to constrain leverage in the banking sector and mitigate destabilising deleveraging processes.
subpolicies.control: Leverage ratio calculated comparably across jurisdictions to supplement risk-based measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mitigating the procyclical amplification of financial shocks throughout the banking system.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of countercyclical buffers and adjustments to accounting standards.

policies.policy_description: Measures to address procyclicality and raise the resilience of the banking sector.
policies.scope: Banking sector
policies.objective: Ensure the banking sector serves as a shock absorber and protects against excess credit growth.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Safeguards to address excess cyclicality of the minimum capital requirement.
subpolicies.control: Use long-term data horizons, downturn loss-given-default estimates, and conduct stress tests.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promoting stronger provisioning practices through expected loss accounting standards.
subpolicies.control: Update supervisory guidance and address incentives in the regulatory capital framework.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Framework to promote conservation of capital and build adequate buffers.
subpolicies.control: Implement internationally agreed capital conservation standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regime to adjust capital buffer range during periods of excessive credit growth.
subpolicies.control: Establish a countercyclical buffer to protect the banking sector.

policies.policy_description: Establishes measures to address unexpected losses and systemic risks in the banking sector.
policies.scope: Banking institutions globally
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain strong capital buffers and address systemic risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must maintain capital buffers above the minimum requirement to protect against severe shocks.
subpolicies.control: Supervisors will monitor compliance and assess capital adequacy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Systemically important banks should have loss absorbing capacity beyond minimum standards.
subpolicies.control: Ongoing development of methodologies to assess systemic importance and additional loss absorbency.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduction of globally harmonized liquidity standards to ensure banks manage liquidity prudently.
subpolicies.control: Supervisors will coordinate follow-up to ensure adherence to liquidity risk management principles.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promotes resilience to liquidity disruptions over a thirty-day horizon.
subpolicies.control: Banks must hold sufficient high-quality liquid assets to offset net cash outflows in stress scenarios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires banks to maintain a minimum amount of stable funding relative to liquidity profiles of assets.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of funding sources to ensure stability and resilience.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the components and requirements for capital within banking organizations.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banking organizations under Basel III standards.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain sufficient capital to support their operations and absorb losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the elements that constitute total regulatory capital, including Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Includes criteria for classification and regulatory adjustments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Sets minimum thresholds for Common Equity Tier 1, Tier 1 Capital, and Total Capital as a percentage of risk-weighted assets.
subpolicies.control: Common Equity Tier 1 must be at least 4.5%, Tier 1 Capital at least 6.0%, and Total Capital at least 8.0%.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the metrics and tools used for monitoring liquidity risk profiles of banking organizations.
subpolicies.control: Includes metrics such as contractual maturity mismatch, concentration of funding, and available unencumbered assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Describes the transitional measures for implementing new capital standards to support lending.
subpolicies.control: Includes observation periods and rigorous reporting processes.

policies.policy_description: Criteria and definitions for Common Equity Tier 1 and Additional Tier 1 capital for banks.
policies.scope: Applicable to internationally active banks and their capital instruments.
policies.objective: To ensure that capital instruments meet specific criteria for classification as Common Equity Tier 1 and Additional Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Common Equity Tier 1 Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria that instruments must meet to be classified as Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be common shares, meet loss absorption criteria, and not create expectations of redemption.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum criteria for instruments to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be subordinated, perpetual, and callable only under specific conditions.

policies.policy_description: Outlines the criteria and requirements for capital instruments under Basel III.
policies.scope: Applicable to banking organizations and their capital instruments.
policies.objective: To ensure that capital instruments meet the necessary requirements for inclusion in Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments classified as liabilities must have principal loss absorption and cannot have credit sensitive dividend features.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must not contribute to liabilities exceeding assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Tier 2 capital, including subordination and maturity requirements.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be issued and paid-in, subordinated to depositors, and not secured.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Dividends must be paid out of distributable items and cannot have features that require payments in kind.
subpolicies.control: Full discretion at all times to cancel distributions/payments is required.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments with features that obligate payment if another instrument is paid are prohibited.
subpolicies.control: Obligation to make payments inconsistent with full discretion.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Stock surplus from instruments included in Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital must meet specific criteria.
subpolicies.control: Stock surplus not eligible for Tier 1 can only be included in Tier 2 if the originating shares are eligible.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provisions for unidentified losses are eligible for inclusion in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Provisions must be freely available to meet future losses.

policies.policy_description: Regulations regarding the inclusion of provisions and minority interests in Tier 2 and Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks calculating capital under the standardised and Internal Ratings-based approaches.
policies.objective: To define the criteria for the inclusion of provisions and minority interests in capital calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limits on general provisions/general loan-loss reserves eligible for inclusion in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Maximum of 1.25 percentage points of credit risk-weighted risk assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which the difference between total expected loss and total eligible provisions can be recognized in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Up to a maximum of 0.6% of credit risk-weighted assets, with a potential lower limit at national discretion.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognizing minority interest in Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Minority interest from common shares issued by fully consolidated subsidiaries must meet specific criteria.

policies.policy_description: Framework for enhancing the resilience of banks and banking systems through improved capital requirements and risk management.
policies.scope: International banking institutions
policies.objective: To strengthen the risk coverage and capital requirements of banks to mitigate systemic risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Improvements to the capital framework to capture major on- and off-balance sheet risks and derivative-related exposures.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of higher capital requirements for trading books and complex securitisation exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Strengthening capital requirements for counterparty credit exposures arising from derivatives and securities financing activities.
subpolicies.control: Introduction of stressed inputs for capital requirements and capital charges for credit valuation adjustment risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduction of a leverage ratio to constrain excessive leverage in the banking sector.
subpolicies.control: Calculation of leverage ratio across jurisdictions to supplement risk-based measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Measures to mitigate the procyclical amplification of financial shocks in the banking system.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of countercyclical buffers and adjustments to accounting standards.

policies.policy_description: Measures to make banks more resilient to procyclical dynamics and ensure the banking sector serves as a shock absorber.
policies.scope: Banking sector
policies.objective: To dampen excess cyclicality of the minimum capital requirement and promote forward-looking provisions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Safeguards to address excess cyclicality of minimum capital requirements, including long-term data horizons and stress testing.
subpolicies.control: Use of long-term data for estimating probabilities of default and conducting stress tests.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promotion of stronger provisioning practices through an expected loss approach and updated supervisory guidance.
subpolicies.control: Advocacy for changes in accounting standards and supervisory guidance to support expected loss provisioning.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Framework to promote the conservation of capital and build adequate buffers above minimum requirements.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of internationally agreed capital conservation standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjustment of capital buffer range in response to signs of excessive credit growth to protect the banking sector.
subpolicies.control: Countercyclical buffer mechanism to build capital defenses during periods of excess credit growth.

policies.policy_description: Focus on unexpected losses with a distinction between addressing cyclicality of the minimum and building additional buffers above that minimum.
policies.scope: Banking sector
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain strong capital buffers to protect against severe shocks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement to address excess credit growth set at zero in normal times, increasing during periods of excessive credit availability.
subpolicies.control: Supervisors expect banks to build a buffer above the minimum.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Systemically important banks should have loss absorbing capacity beyond minimum standards.
subpolicies.control: Ongoing work by the Basel Committee and Financial Stability Board on capital surcharges and contingent capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Capital requirements introduced to mitigate risks arising from firm-level exposures among global financial institutions.
subpolicies.control: Includes capital incentives for using central counterparties and higher capital requirements for trading and derivative activities.

policies.policy_description: Introduction of internationally harmonised global liquidity standards to promote banking sector stability.
policies.scope: Banking sector
policies.objective: To establish minimum liquidity requirements and promote an international level playing field.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promotes resilience to potential liquidity disruptions over a thirty-day horizon.
subpolicies.control: Ensures banks have sufficient high-quality liquid assets to offset net cash outflows under stress scenarios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires a minimum amount of stable sources of funding relative to the liquidity profiles of the assets.
subpolicies.control: Promotes a sustainable maturity structure of assets and liabilities.

policies.policy_description: Aims to limit over-reliance on short-term wholesale funding and encourage better assessment of liquidity risk across all on- and off-balance sheet items.
policies.scope: Banking organizations and financial sector supervision
policies.objective: To ensure stability in funding and liquidity risk management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks should frequently conduct assessments to understand their liquidity needs and compare liquidity risk profiles.
subpolicies.control: Regular assessments to highlight potential liquidity needs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Analyzes concentrations of wholesale funding to assess potential funding liquidity risks.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of funding sources and their withdrawal impacts.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Measures the amount of unencumbered assets available for secured funding.
subpolicies.control: Awareness of capacity to raise additional secured funds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Assesses LCR in each significant currency to monitor foreign exchange risk as a component of liquidity risk.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring and managing currency exposure.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Utilizes market-wide data to monitor potential liquidity difficulties.
subpolicies.control: Analysis of asset prices, liquidity, and institution-specific funding information.

policies.policy_description: Introduces transitional arrangements to implement new standards for higher capital requirements.
policies.scope: Banking sector during the transition to higher capital standards.
policies.objective: To ensure banks can meet capital standards while supporting lending.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: LCR to be introduced on 1 January 2015 and NSFR by 1 January 2018.
subpolicies.control: Rigorous reporting processes to monitor ratios during the transition.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Both LCR and NSFR will have observation periods and review clauses to address unintended consequences.
subpolicies.control: Ongoing review of implications for financial markets and economic growth.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the components and criteria for regulatory capital.
policies.scope: Banks and banking groups measuring capital.
policies.objective: To ensure consistency and quality in capital definitions across jurisdictions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines components of Tier 1 Capital including Common Equity Tier 1 and Additional Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Minimum requirements of 4.5% for Common Equity Tier 1 and 6.0% for Tier 1 Capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines criteria for Tier 2 Capital as gone-concern capital.
subpolicies.control: Minimum requirements for Total Capital of 8.0% of risk-weighted assets.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to internationally active banks structured as joint stock companies.
policies.objective: To ensure that instruments classified as Common Equity Tier 1 meet specific criteria for loss absorption and capital quality.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Classification as Common Shares
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the characteristics that must be met for an instrument to be classified as common shares for regulatory capital purposes.
subpolicies.control: Includes criteria such as subordinated claims, perpetual principal, and non-obligatory distributions.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to instruments issued by the bank and its consolidated subsidiaries.
policies.objective: To outline the minimum criteria for instruments to qualify as Additional Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Inclusion in Additional Tier 1 Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the minimum set of criteria for an instrument to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Includes requirements such as being issued and paid-in, subordinated, perpetual, and having dividend discretion.

policies.policy_description: Regulations and requirements governing the components of capital for banking organizations.
policies.scope: Applicable to banking organizations and their capital instruments.
policies.objective: To ensure that capital instruments meet specific criteria for inclusion in Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Dividends/coupons must be paid out of distributable items and cannot have credit sensitive features.
subpolicies.control: Prohibits dividend pushers and requires full discretion to cancel distributions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments classified as liabilities must have principal loss absorption mechanisms.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Classification as Common Shares
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria that must be met for an instrument to be classified as common shares for regulatory capital purposes.
subpolicies.control: Includes criteria such as perpetual principal, non-obligatory distributions, and loss absorption features.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to instruments issued by the bank and its consolidated subsidiaries.
policies.objective: To define the minimum criteria for inclusion in Additional Tier 1 capital to support the bank's financial resilience.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Inclusion in Additional Tier 1 Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum set of criteria for an instrument issued by the bank to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Includes requirements such as being issued and paid-in, subordinated, perpetual, and having discretion over distributions.

policies.policy_description: Regulations regarding the payment of dividends and coupons to common stockholders.
policies.scope: Applicable to banking organizations and their capital instruments.
policies.objective: To ensure that dividends and distributions are paid out of distributable items and do not compromise the bank's financial stability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Dividends/coupons must be paid out of distributable items.
subpolicies.control: Ensures compliance with financial regulations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot have a credit sensitive dividend feature.
subpolicies.control: Prevents obligations based on the bank's credit standing.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must not contribute to liabilities exceeding assets as per insolvency law.
subpolicies.control: Maintains financial soundness.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Replacement issues can occur concurrently with but not after the instrument is called.
subpolicies.control: Regulates timing of capital replacement.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum refers to the regulator’s prescribed minimum requirement, which may exceed Basel III requirements.
subpolicies.control: Ensures adherence to regulatory standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Full discretion to cancel distributions/payments prohibits 'dividend pushers'.
subpolicies.control: Ensures flexibility in capital management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments classified as liabilities must have principal loss absorption through conversion or write-down mechanisms.
subpolicies.control: Facilitates loss absorption in adverse conditions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Neither the bank nor related parties can purchase the instrument or fund its purchase.
subpolicies.control: Prevents conflicts of interest.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must not have features that hinder recapitalisation.
subpolicies.control: Ensures flexibility for future capital raising.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Proceeds from instruments not issued by an operating entity must be immediately available to the operating entity.
subpolicies.control: Ensures liquidity and compliance with capital requirements.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Tier 2 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to banking organizations and their capital instruments.
policies.objective: To provide loss absorption on a gone-concern basis.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Inclusion in Tier 2 Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must be issued and paid-in, subordinated, and meet specific maturity requirements.
subpolicies.control: Ensures that Tier 2 capital is robust and reliable.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments may be callable only after a minimum of five years with prior supervisory approval.
subpolicies.control: Regulates the timing and conditions under which calls can be exercised.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investors must not have rights to accelerate repayment except in bankruptcy or liquidation.
subpolicies.control: Protects the bank's capital structure.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot have a credit sensitive dividend feature.
subpolicies.control: Ensures stability in dividend payments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Neither the bank nor related parties can purchase the instrument or fund its purchase.
subpolicies.control: Prevents conflicts of interest.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Proceeds from instruments not issued by an operating entity must be immediately available to the operating entity.
subpolicies.control: Ensures liquidity and compliance with capital requirements.

policies.policy_title: Loan-Loss Reserves Inclusion in Tier 2 Capital
policies.policy_description: Regulations regarding the inclusion of general provisions and loan-loss reserves in Tier 2 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks calculating credit risk-weighted assets under the standardized and Internal Ratings-based approaches.
policies.objective: To limit the inclusion of loan-loss reserves in Tier 2 capital to ensure financial stability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: General provisions eligible for inclusion in Tier 2 are limited to a maximum of 1.25 percentage points of credit risk-weighted risk assets.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with the limit on general provisions as per the standardised approach.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Recognition of Excess Provisions under IRB Approach
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If total expected loss is less than total eligible provisions, the difference may be recognized in Tier 2 capital up to a maximum of 0.6% of credit risk-weighted assets.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring and reporting of expected losses and eligible provisions under the Internal Ratings-based Approach.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minority interest from common shares issued by fully consolidated subsidiaries may receive recognition in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of minority interests in consolidated subsidiaries for Tier 2 capital recognition.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory framework for capitalisation of bank exposures to central counterparties and risk management standards.
policies.scope: Banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To enhance the resilience of banks and mitigate systemic risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Capitalisation of Bank Exposures to CCPs
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Capitalisation based on compliance of CCPs with IOSCO standards, with low risk weights for compliant exposures.
subpolicies.control: Risk weight of 2% for collateral and mark-to-market exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Raising standards for managing counterparty credit risk, including wrong-way risk.
subpolicies.control: Final guidance for sound backtesting of counterparty credit exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Internal Assessment of Externally Rated Securitisation Exposures
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for banks to perform internal assessments and mitigate reliance on external ratings.
subpolicies.control: Elimination of cliff effects and incorporation of IOSCO Code of Conduct elements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduction of a leverage ratio to constrain excessive leverage in the banking sector.
subpolicies.control: Calculation of leverage ratio across jurisdictions with adjustments for accounting standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduction of measures to mitigate procyclical dynamics and enhance banking sector resilience.
subpolicies.control: Dampening excess cyclicality of minimum capital requirements and promoting forward-looking provisions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement for banks to conduct stress tests considering credit portfolio migration during recessions.
subpolicies.control: Use of long-term data horizons and downturn loss-given-default estimates.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing Additional Tier 1 capital in banking systems.
policies.scope: Banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To enhance the resilience of banks and banking systems through improved capital management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promoting stronger provisioning practices through an expected loss approach.
subpolicies.control: Guiding principles for the revision of accounting standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Framework to promote conservation of capital and build adequate buffers.
subpolicies.control: Internationally agreed capital conservation standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjusting capital buffer ranges in response to excessive credit growth.
subpolicies.control: Countercyclical buffer mechanism.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Developing an integrated approach to manage systemic risks among important financial institutions.
subpolicies.control: Methodology for assessing systemic importance and additional loss absorbency.

policies.policy_description: Framework for Tier 2 capital under Basel III to enhance banking sector resilience.
policies.scope: Banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To establish minimum capital and liquidity requirements to ensure banking stability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promotes resilience to potential liquidity disruptions over a thirty-day horizon by ensuring sufficient high-quality liquid assets.
subpolicies.control: Requires banks to maintain unencumbered, high-quality liquid assets to offset net cash outflows under stress scenarios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires a minimum amount of stable sources of funding relative to the liquidity profiles of assets over a one-year horizon.
subpolicies.control: Aims to limit reliance on short-term wholesale funding and encourages better liquidity risk assessment.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Develops common metrics for supervisors to monitor liquidity risk profiles consistently across jurisdictions.
subpolicies.control: Introduces a set of minimum types of information for supervisors to use while allowing additional metrics for jurisdiction-specific risks.

sections.title: consolidated subsidiaries that is held by third parties

policies.policy_description: A global regulatory framework for more resilient banks and banking systems focusing on liquidity risk management.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions under Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To enhance the resilience of banks by ensuring they maintain adequate liquidity levels.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks should frequently conduct assessments to understand their liquidity needs based on contractual commitments.
subpolicies.control: Regular assessments to compare liquidity risk profiles across institutions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Analysis of concentrations of wholesale funding from specific counterparties, instruments, and currencies.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring to assess potential funding liquidity risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Measurement of unencumbered assets available for collateral in secured funding.
subpolicies.control: Awareness of capacity to raise additional secured funds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Assessment of LCR in significant currencies to manage currency exposure.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring and managing overall currency exposure levels.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Utilization of market-wide data and institution-specific information to monitor liquidity difficulties.
subpolicies.control: Access to instantaneous data on asset prices and liquidity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduction of transitional arrangements to implement new capital standards while supporting lending.
subpolicies.control: Rigorous reporting processes to monitor ratios during the transition period.

policies.policy_title: Scope of Application of Minimum Capital Requirements
policies.policy_description: Defines the scope of application for minimum capital requirements as per Basel II framework.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks and financial institutions under Basel II framework.
policies.objective: To ensure consistency in the application of capital requirements across institutions.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory adjustments are subject to specific restrictions regarding capital requirements.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and banking groups.
policies.objective: To ensure minimum capital requirements are maintained for financial stability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Common Equity Tier 1 must be at least 4.5% of risk-weighted assets at all times.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with minimum capital ratios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Tier 1 Capital must be at least 6.0% of risk-weighted assets at all times.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of Tier 1 capital levels.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Total Capital (Tier 1 Capital plus Tier 2 Capital) must be at least 8.0% of risk-weighted assets at all times.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of total capital adequacy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Common Equity Tier 1 Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the elements that constitute Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Verification of compliance with classification criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the elements that constitute Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of instruments for inclusion in Additional Tier 1 capital.

policies.policy_title: Criteria for Inclusion in Additional Tier 1 Capital
policies.policy_description: Minimum set of criteria for an instrument issued by the bank to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Instruments issued by the bank
policies.objective: To ensure that instruments meet the necessary criteria for inclusion in Additional Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must be issued and paid-in.
subpolicies.control: Must meet issuance requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must be subordinated to depositors, general creditors, and subordinated debt of the bank.
subpolicies.control: Must maintain subordination status.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must not be secured or covered by guarantees that enhance the seniority of the claim.
subpolicies.control: Must avoid any security or guarantee features.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must be perpetual with no maturity date or incentives to redeem.
subpolicies.control: Must not include maturity or redemption incentives.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments may be callable only after five years with prior supervisory approval.
subpolicies.control: Must not create expectations of call exercise.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Any repayment must have prior supervisory approval and should not create market expectations.
subpolicies.control: Must adhere to approval requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The bank must have full discretion to cancel distributions/payments.
subpolicies.control: Cancellation must not be an event of default.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Dividends/coupons must be paid out of distributable items.
