subpolicies.control: Must ensure payments are from eligible items.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot have a credit sensitive dividend feature.
subpolicies.control: Must avoid credit sensitivity in dividends.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot contribute to liabilities exceeding assets.
subpolicies.control: Must comply with national insolvency law.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must have principal loss absorption through conversion or write-down mechanisms.
subpolicies.control: Must specify trigger points for loss absorption.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Neither the bank nor related parties can purchase the instrument or fund its purchase.
subpolicies.control: Must ensure compliance with purchase restrictions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot have features that hinder recapitalisation.
subpolicies.control: Must avoid compensation provisions for lower-priced instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If not issued from an operating entity, proceeds must be immediately available to an operating entity.
subpolicies.control: Must ensure immediate availability of proceeds.

policies.policy_description: Criteria and components for Tier 2 capital.
policies.scope: Instruments and provisions related to Tier 2 capital.
policies.objective: To define the elements that constitute Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments issued by the bank that meet the criteria for inclusion in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Must not be included in Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Stock surplus resulting from the issue of instruments included in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Must comply with eligibility requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments issued by consolidated subsidiaries that meet Tier 2 criteria.
subpolicies.control: Must not be included in Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Certain loan loss provisions as specified in regulatory guidelines.
subpolicies.control: Must adhere to specified paragraphs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjustments applied in the calculation of Tier 2 Capital.
subpolicies.control: Must comply with regulatory requirements.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for an instrument to be included in Tier 2 capital as per Basel III regulations.
policies.scope: Instruments issued by banks
policies.objective: To ensure loss absorption on a gone-concern basis.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must be issued and paid-in.
subpolicies.control: Must be subordinated to depositors and general creditors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must not be secured or guaranteed in a way that enhances seniority.
subpolicies.control: No legal or economic enhancements to the claim.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must have a minimum original maturity of five years and specific amortization conditions.
subpolicies.control: No step-ups or incentives to redeem.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments may be callable only after five years with supervisory approval and conditions.
subpolicies.control: Must not create an expectation of call exercise.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investors must not have rights to accelerate repayment except in bankruptcy.
subpolicies.control: No acceleration rights except in liquidation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot have credit-sensitive dividend features.
subpolicies.control: Dividends must not be based on the bank's credit standing.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The bank or related parties cannot purchase the instrument or fund its purchase.
subpolicies.control: No direct or indirect funding of purchases.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Proceeds from instruments not issued out of operating entities must be available to operating entities.
subpolicies.control: Must meet all other Tier 2 criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Stock surplus from Tier 2 instruments can only be included if the shares meet Tier 2 criteria.
subpolicies.control: Only eligible stock surplus is permitted.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: General provisions can be included in Tier 2 capital under specific conditions.
subpolicies.control: Limited to a maximum of 1.25 percentage points of credit risk-weighted assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recognition of excess provisions under the Internal Ratings-based Approach.
subpolicies.control: Up to a maximum of 0.6% of credit risk-weighted assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minority interest from common shares issued by subsidiaries may be recognized in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Must meet all criteria if issued by the bank.

policies.policy_description: Framework for credit protection and recognition of ratings in banking systems.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions under Basel III guidelines.
policies.objective: To ensure resilience in banks and proper risk management through credit protection and rating recognition.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognizing credit protection provided to securitisation exposures.
subpolicies.control: Entities must be externally rated BBB- or better at the time of credit protection provision.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for banks on the use of ratings from External Credit Assessment Institutions (ECAIs).
subpolicies.control: Banks must use chosen ECAIs consistently and are not allowed to cherry-pick ratings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operational guidelines for maintaining capital buffers outside periods of stress.
subpolicies.control: Banks should hold capital buffers above regulatory minimums and manage distributions of earnings accordingly.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recommendations for banks on rebuilding capital buffers after drawdowns.
subpolicies.control: Banks should consider reducing discretionary distributions and may raise new capital from the private sector.

policies.policy_description: Framework for assessing and recognizing capital instruments under Basel III.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and their consolidated subsidiaries.
policies.objective: To ensure that capital instruments meet regulatory requirements for classification and recognition.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Recognition of Minority Interest in Common Equity Tier 1
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria and calculation for recognizing minority interest in consolidated Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Calculation involves total minority interest minus surplus Common Equity Tier 1 attributable to minority shareholders.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognizing Tier 1 capital instruments issued by consolidated subsidiaries.
subpolicies.control: Calculation involves total Tier 1 of the subsidiary minus surplus Tier 1 attributable to third party investors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognizing total capital instruments issued by consolidated subsidiaries.
subpolicies.control: Calculation involves total capital instruments of the subsidiary minus surplus Total Capital attributable to third party investors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Treatment of Capital Issued via Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs)
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which capital issued through SPVs may be included in Additional Tier 1 or Tier 2.
subpolicies.control: Capital can be included if it meets entry criteria and the SPV's only asset is its investment in the bank's capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjustments to be applied to regulatory capital calculations.
subpolicies.control: Includes deductions for goodwill, intangibles, and deferred tax assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deduction requirements for goodwill and other intangibles in Common Equity Tier 1 calculation.
subpolicies.control: Full amount deducted net of associated deferred tax liability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for deducting deferred tax assets that rely on future profitability.
subpolicies.control: DTAs may be netted with associated DTLs only if permitted by the relevant taxation authority.

policies.policy_description: Policies related to the coverage of risks associated with operating losses, tax assets, and capital calculations.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To ensure accurate calculation of Common Equity Tier 1 and manage risks associated with tax assets and provisions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Deduction of Unused Tax Losses and Credits
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Unused tax losses and credits are to be deducted in full net of deferred tax liabilities.
subpolicies.control: Ensure DTLs excluded from netting against goodwill, intangibles, and pension assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Claims or receivables from tax authorities due to overinstallment or carryback of current year tax losses are classified as current tax assets.
subpolicies.control: Assign relevant sovereign risk weighting.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Cash flow hedge reserve related to non-fair valued items should be derecognised in Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.
subpolicies.control: Adjust for positive and negative amounts accordingly.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Shortfall of Provisions to Expected Losses
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Full deduction from capital for shortfalls in provisions under the IRB approach.
subpolicies.control: No reduction for expected tax effects.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Derecognise any increase in equity capital from securitisation transactions in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Exclude expected future margin income.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Unrealised gains and losses from changes in fair value of liabilities due to own credit risk must be derecognised.
subpolicies.control: Apply to Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Defined Benefit Pension Fund Assets and Liabilities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Fully recognise defined benefit pension fund liabilities in Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.
subpolicies.control: Deduct pension fund assets net of associated deferred tax liabilities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All investments in own common shares must be deducted in Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.
subpolicies.control: Apply deductions irrespective of exposure location.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reciprocal cross holdings designed to inflate capital positions must be deducted in full.
subpolicies.control: Apply corresponding deduction approach.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Investments in Capital of Other Entities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investments in capital of banking and financial entities outside regulatory consolidation must be adjusted.
subpolicies.control: Include all direct, indirect, and synthetic holdings.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory adjustments related to capital holdings and investments in financial institutions.
policies.scope: Applies to banks and financial institutions regarding their capital holdings.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks maintain adequate capital levels and manage risk associated with investments in other financial entities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deduction of amounts exceeding 10% of the bank’s common equity from respective capital tiers.
subpolicies.control: Shortfalls in capital deductions are to be deducted from the next higher tier of capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limited recognition for certain items when calculating Common Equity Tier 1, capped at 10% of common equity.
subpolicies.control: Items include significant investments in common shares of unconsolidated financial institutions, mortgage servicing rights, and deferred tax assets from temporary differences.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for excluding certain investments from regulatory capital calculations with supervisory approval.
subpolicies.control: Investments made in the context of resolving or providing financial assistance to distressed institutions may be excluded.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allow banks to use conservative estimates for exposures to capital of other financial institutions under specific conditions.
subpolicies.control: Subject to prior supervisory approval.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing risk coverage related to capital requirements and regulatory adjustments.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions under Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital levels and improve transparency in regulatory capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Items included in the 15% aggregate limit must not exceed 15% of Common Equity Tier 1 capital after regulatory adjustments.
subpolicies.control: Full disclosure of items subject to the 15% limit.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Items not deducted from Common Equity Tier 1 will be risk weighted at 250%.
subpolicies.control: Risk weighting applied to specific investment types.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must disclose regulatory capital elements, adjustments, limits, and features of capital instruments.
subpolicies.control: Transparency measures for regulatory capital disclosures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Phased implementation of new capital standards and adjustments from 2013 to 2018.
subpolicies.control: Gradual increase in minimum capital requirements and adjustments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulatory adjustments will be fully deducted from Common Equity Tier 1 by 2018, with gradual increases starting in 2014.
subpolicies.control: Schedule for implementing deductions from capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments that no longer qualify as Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital will be phased out starting 2013.
subpolicies.control: Capping recognition of non-qualifying instruments.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing risk coverage in regulatory capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to capital instruments and counterparty credit risk.
policies.objective: To ensure all material risks are captured in the capital framework and to delineate the treatment of capital instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments not called at effective maturity date and not meeting new criteria will be derecognised from regulatory capital.
subpolicies.control: Effective from 1 January 2013.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments that no longer qualify as Additional Tier 1 or Tier 2 will be phased out from 1 January 2013.
subpolicies.control: Applicable to instruments issued before 12 September 2010.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reforms to the counterparty credit risk framework effective from 1 January 2013.
subpolicies.control: Includes revised metrics for counterparty credit risk and capital charge calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Capital charge for mark-to-market losses on expected counterparty risk (CVA) to OTC derivatives.
subpolicies.control: Calculated based on the bank’s method for counterparty credit risk.

policies.policy_title: Market Risk Internal Models Approach for CVA Calculation
policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for calculating the capital charge for counterparty credit risk using the market risk internal models approach.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks calculating CVA for OTC derivative counterparties.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate calculation of CVA capital charges based on credit spread changes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the formula and methodology for calculating the CVA of each counterparty, including the use of credit spreads and expected exposures.
subpolicies.control: Must adhere to the specified formula and parameters for CVA calculation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies the requirement to use CDS spreads when available, or appropriate proxy spreads based on counterparty characteristics.
subpolicies.control: Ensure the use of appropriate spreads for CVA calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the approach for determining the loss given default for counterparties based on market instruments or proxy spreads.
subpolicies.control: Differentiate between market assessment and internal estimates for LGD.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the calculation of expected exposure at revaluation times, including the aggregation of exposures across netting sets.
subpolicies.control: Follow regulatory definitions for expected exposure.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies the requirements for the bank's approved VaR model, including the use of specific formulas for credit spread sensitivities.
subpolicies.control: Ensure compliance with the approved VaR model parameters.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: CVA Risk Capital Charge for Non-IMM Netting Sets
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the treatment of non-IMM netting sets in the CVA risk capital charge calculation.
subpolicies.control: Apply constant EE profiles as specified.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Describes the conditions under which the standardized method must be used for calculating CVA risk charges for specific exposures.
subpolicies.control: Obtain supervisory approval for modeling specific risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the components of the CVA risk capital charge, including general and specific credit spread risks.
subpolicies.control: Calculate VaR according to specified standards.

policies.policy_description: Framework for calculating the CVA risk capital charge for banks based on exposure parameters and eligible hedges.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks calculating CVA risk capital charges.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks maintain adequate capital against CVA risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines which hedges can be included in the CVA risk capital charge calculation.
subpolicies.control: Only single-name CDSs, single-name contingent CDSs, and index CDSs are eligible, with specific conditions on their use.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Calculation Method for Standardised CVA Risk Capital Charge
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the formula and parameters for banks without approval to use the advanced CVA capital charge calculation.
subpolicies.control: Banks must follow the specified formula using weights based on external ratings and exposure at default.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Aggregation of CCR and CVA Risk Capital Charges
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the aggregation process for default risk capital charge and CVA risk capital charge.
subpolicies.control: Banks with IMM approval must calculate total CCR capital charge based on specified components.

policies.policy_description: Framework for assessing and managing risk coverage in banking systems.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions under Basel III regulations.
policies.objective: To ensure banks adequately cover risks associated with counterparty credit exposures and wrong-way risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: CCR Capital Charge for Banks with IMM Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determines total CCR capital charge for banks with Internal Models Method (IMM) approval without Specific Risk VaR approval for bonds.
subpolicies.control: Sum of the higher IMM capital charge based on current or stressed parameter calibrations and the standardized CVA risk capital charge.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: CCR Capital Charge for All Other Banks
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determines total CCR capital charge for banks not covered by specific IMM approvals.
subpolicies.control: Sum over all counterparties of the CEM or SM based capital charge and the standardized CVA risk capital charge.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines 'Outstanding EAD' for OTC derivative counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Calculated as the greater of zero and the difference between total EADs and recognized CVA losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires banks to identify exposures that give rise to greater general wrong-way risk.
subpolicies.control: Stress testing and scenario analyses to monitor wrong-way risks by various categories.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines requirements for managing specific wrong-way risk in EAD calculations.
subpolicies.control: Policies for identifying and treating exposures with specific wrong-way risk differently.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Policies regarding the treatment of individual entities in connected groups.
subpolicies.control: Must include processes for identifying specific wrong-way risk for each legal entity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: EAD Calculation for Specific Wrong-Way Risk
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the calculation of EAD for transactions with identified specific wrong-way risk.
subpolicies.control: EAD equals the full expected loss in the remaining fair value of underlying instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: LGD for Advanced or Foundation IRB Banks
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Sets LGD to 100% for transactions identified with specific wrong-way risk.
subpolicies.control: Ensures that capital requirements reflect the full risk of such exposures.

policies.policy_description: Framework for calculating risk-weighted assets and capital requirements for financial institutions.
policies.scope: Large financial institutions and regulated financial institutions.
policies.objective: To implement the AVC multiplier and adjust capital requirements based on risk exposure.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A multiplier of 1.25 is applied to the correlation parameter for regulated financial institutions with total assets greater than or equal to US $100 billion.
subpolicies.control: Use the most recent audited financial statement to determine asset size.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Margin Period of Risk for Collateralised Counterparties
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: New supervisory floors for margin periods based on transaction types and conditions.
subpolicies.control: Impose a supervisory floor of five business days for repo-style transactions and ten business days for other transactions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Increased Margin Period for High Dispute Netting Sets
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If a bank has more than two margin call disputes on a netting set, the margin period of risk must be at least double the supervisory floor for the subsequent two quarters.
subpolicies.control: Reflect the history of margin call disputes in the margin period of risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjustments to minimum holding periods for various transaction types based on daily re-margining.
subpolicies.control: Minimum holding periods are defined for repo-style transactions, capital market transactions, and secured lending.

policies.policy_description: Policy outlining the minimum holding period for repo-style transactions and netting sets based on risk criteria.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks engaging in repo-style transactions or similar netting sets.
policies.objective: To establish a minimum holding period that reflects the liquidity of the instrument and the margin period of risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Minimum Holding Period for Repo Transactions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the minimum holding period as the margin period of risk applicable under specified paragraphs of Annex 4.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with paragraphs 41(i), 41(ii), and 41(iii) of Annex 4.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Revision of the shortcut method for estimating Effective EPE for banks without margin agreements.
subpolicies.control: Adherence to the revised guidelines in paragraph 41 of Annex 4 in Basel II.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing counterparty credit risk in accordance with Basel III regulations.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and their consolidated subsidiaries.
policies.objective: To ensure proper classification and recognition of capital instruments for regulatory purposes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Recognition of Minority Interest in Common Equity Tier 1
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria and calculation for recognizing minority interest in consolidated Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Calculation of total minority interest minus surplus attributable to minority shareholders.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognition of Tier 1 capital instruments issued by consolidated subsidiaries.
subpolicies.control: Calculation of total Tier 1 minus surplus attributable to third party investors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognition of total capital instruments issued by consolidated subsidiaries.
subpolicies.control: Calculation of total capital instruments minus surplus attributable to third party investors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which capital issued through SPVs can be included in Additional Tier 1 or Tier 2.
subpolicies.control: Treatment of capital as if issued directly by the bank under specific conditions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjustments to be applied to regulatory capital calculations.
subpolicies.control: Deduction of goodwill, intangibles, and deferred tax assets from Common Equity Tier 1.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for deducting goodwill and other intangible assets from Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Full deduction of goodwill and intangibles, net of deferred tax liabilities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for deducting deferred tax assets that rely on future profitability.
subpolicies.control: Deduction of DTAs that do not meet specific criteria for realization.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing counterparty credit risk related to capital calculations and deductions.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks under Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate calculation of Common Equity Tier 1 and mitigate risks associated with counterparty credit.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deferred tax assets must be deducted in full net of deferred tax liabilities, excluding amounts netted against goodwill and intangibles.
subpolicies.control: Pro rata allocation of deferred tax liabilities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Positive amounts in cash flow hedge reserves should be deducted, while negative amounts should be added back in Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.
subpolicies.control: Identification of elements causing volatility in common equity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Shortfall of Provisions to Expected Losses
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Full deduction from capital for any shortfall of provisions under the IRB approach.
subpolicies.control: No reduction for expected tax effects.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Gain on Sale from Securitisation Transactions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Any increase in equity capital from securitisation transactions must be derecognised in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Ensure accurate reflection of capital changes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defined benefit pension fund liabilities must be fully recognised, and assets deducted net of deferred tax liabilities.
subpolicies.control: Offsetting assets with supervisory approval.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All investments in a bank’s own common shares must be deducted in Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.
subpolicies.control: Avoid double counting of capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reciprocal cross holdings designed to inflate capital positions must be deducted in full.
subpolicies.control: Corresponding deduction approach applied.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Investments in Capital of Other Entities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investments in capital of banking, financial, and insurance entities outside regulatory consolidation must be included in capital calculations.
subpolicies.control: Net long positions to be included, with specific exclusions for short-term underwriting.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory adjustments related to capital holdings and deductions for banks in relation to counterparty credit risk.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks regarding their capital holdings and investments in financial institutions.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks maintain adequate capital levels and manage risks associated with counterparty credit exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjustments to Common Equity Tier 1 capital based on holdings exceeding 10% of the bank's common equity.
subpolicies.control: Deductions applied to the same component of capital for which the capital would qualify if issued by the bank.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Additional Tier 1 and Tier 2 Capital Adjustments
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjustments to Additional Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital based on holdings exceeding 10% of the bank's common equity.
subpolicies.control: Deductions calculated based on the proportion of total capital holdings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limited recognition for certain investments when calculating Common Equity Tier 1, capped at 10% of the bank's common equity.
subpolicies.control: Recognition capped for significant investments in unconsolidated financial institutions, mortgage servicing rights, and deferred tax assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provision for banks to use conservative estimates for monitoring exposures if operationally burdensome.
subpolicies.control: Subject to prior supervisory approval.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Shortfalls in capital deductions to be deducted from the next higher tier of capital.
subpolicies.control: If a bank lacks sufficient capital in a tier to satisfy deductions, the shortfall is deducted from the next higher tier.

policies.policy_description: Regulations regarding the management of counterparty credit risk including capital requirements and disclosure obligations.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital against counterparty credit risk and enhance transparency.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Items included in the 15% aggregate limit are subject to full disclosure and must not exceed 15% of Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Regulatory adjustments and risk weighting.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Items not deducted from Common Equity Tier 1 will be risk weighted at 250%.
subpolicies.control: Risk weighting criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must disclose a full reconciliation of regulatory capital elements and other specified disclosures.
subpolicies.control: Transparency and market discipline.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Phased implementation of new capital standards to support lending while meeting higher capital requirements.
subpolicies.control: Timeline for implementation and adjustment percentages.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Capital instruments that no longer qualify will be phased out starting 1 January 2013 with a cap on recognition.
subpolicies.control: Gradual reduction of recognition percentage.

policies.policy_description: Framework outlining the reforms to the counterparty credit risk management and capital requirements.
policies.scope: Applicable to all financial instruments and entities subject to capital requirements.
policies.objective: To ensure that all material risks are captured in the capital framework and to enhance the quality and level of the capital base.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments that do not meet the new criteria for inclusion in Tier 1 or Tier 2 will be derecognised from regulatory capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments not called at their effective maturity date will be phased out from 1 January 2013.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Phasing Out of Additional Tier 1 and Tier 2 Instruments
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments that no longer qualify as Additional Tier 1 or Tier 2 will be phased out based on their issuance date and call features.
subpolicies.control: Instruments issued before 12 September 2010 will qualify for transition arrangements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Capital instruments that do not meet the criteria for inclusion in Common Equity Tier 1 will be excluded from Common Equity Tier 1 as of 1 January 2013.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be issued by non-joint stock companies, treated as equity, and receive unlimited recognition under national banking law.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reforms to ensure that all material on- and off-balance sheet risks, as well as derivative-related exposures, are captured.
subpolicies.control: Effective from 1 January 2013.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory adjustments to be applied to regulatory capital as per Basel III framework.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and banking systems under Basel III regulations.
policies.objective: To ensure the resilience of banks by defining the treatment of capital instruments and adjustments in the calculation of Common Equity Tier 1.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognition of Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital instruments issued by consolidated subsidiaries.
subpolicies.control: Calculation of total capital instruments issued to third parties minus surplus total capital attributable to third party investors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Capital Issued through Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs)
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which capital issued through SPVs can be included in Additional Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Capital can be included if it meets entry criteria and the SPV's only asset is its investment in the bank's capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Full deduction of goodwill and other intangibles in the calculation of Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Deductions are net of associated deferred tax liabilities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deduction of DTAs that rely on future profitability in the calculation of Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: DTAs may be netted with associated DTLs under specific conditions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Derecognition of cash flow hedge reserve amounts related to items not fair valued on the balance sheet.
subpolicies.control: Positive amounts deducted and negative amounts added back in Common Equity Tier 1.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Shortfall of Provisions to Expected Losses
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deduction from capital for shortfall of provisions to expected losses under the IRB approach.
subpolicies.control: Full amount deducted without tax effect reductions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Derecognition of increases in equity capital from securitisation transactions in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Includes gains associated with expected future margin income.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Derecognition of unrealised gains and losses due to changes in the fair value of liabilities from the bank's own credit risk.
subpolicies.control: All such gains and losses must be excluded from Common Equity Tier 1.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Full recognition of defined benefit pension fund liabilities in Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.
subpolicies.control: Assets must be deducted net of associated deferred tax liabilities.

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the treatment of assets and investments in the context of adjustments to capital calculations, focusing on the deduction of certain investments and the management of risk weights.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions regarding their capital calculations.
policies.objective: To ensure proper capital deductions and risk weightings to avoid double counting and manage risks associated with investments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Deduction of Investments in Own Shares
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All investments in a bank's own common shares must be deducted in the calculation of Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Investments include direct, indirect, and contractual obligations to purchase own shares.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reciprocal cross holdings of capital that artificially inflate the capital position must be deducted in full.
subpolicies.control: Apply a corresponding deduction approach to such investments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Investments in Capital of Other Entities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investments in the capital of banking, financial, and insurance entities outside the scope of regulatory consolidation must be deducted if they exceed 10% of the bank's common equity.
subpolicies.control: Include direct, indirect, and synthetic holdings of capital instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If total holdings exceed 10% of common equity, the excess must be deducted according to the corresponding deduction approach.
subpolicies.control: Calculate deductions based on the proportion of total capital holdings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If a bank lacks sufficient capital in a tier to satisfy a deduction, the shortfall will be deducted from the next higher tier of capital.
subpolicies.control: Ensure proper tier deductions to maintain capital adequacy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Amounts below the threshold that are not deducted will continue to be risk weighted according to applicable rules.
subpolicies.control: Instruments in the trading book will follow market risk rules, while those in the banking book will follow the internal ratings-based or standardized approach.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory adjustments related to investments in the capital of banking, financial, and insurance entities outside the scope of regulatory consolidation.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks with significant investments in entities outside regulatory consolidation.
policies.objective: To ensure proper risk weighting and capital allocation for investments in financial entities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Holdings must be allocated on a pro rata basis between those below and above the threshold.
subpolicies.control: Monitor investments in capital instruments, including direct, indirect, and synthetic holdings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Holdings in both banking and trading books must be included, considering net long positions.
subpolicies.control: Assess the maturity of positions to determine inclusion.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Underwriting positions held for five working days or less can be excluded.
subpolicies.control: Track the duration of underwriting positions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: National discretion allows temporary exclusion of certain investments made to assist distressed institutions.
subpolicies.control: Obtain prior supervisory approval for exclusions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investments not qualifying as common shares must be fully deducted from the corresponding tier of capital.
subpolicies.control: Ensure sufficient capital exists to satisfy deduction requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Certain items may receive limited recognition when calculating Common Equity Tier 1, capped at 10% of common equity.
subpolicies.control: Monitor the aggregate of significant investments, MSRs, and DTAs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Items not deducted in the calculation of Common Equity Tier 1 will be risk weighted at 250%.
subpolicies.control: Apply appropriate risk weights to non-deducted items.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must disclose a full reconciliation of regulatory capital elements and specific adjustments.
subpolicies.control: Ensure compliance with disclosure requirements for transparency.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implementation of new standards to meet higher capital requirements starting 1 January 2013.
subpolicies.control: Member countries must translate rules into national laws before the implementation date.

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum capital requirements for banks to enhance resilience.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks and banking systems.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital levels to absorb losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Phased increase in minimum Common Equity Tier 1 requirements from 2013 to 2015.
subpolicies.control: Minimum requirements of 3.5% by 2013, 4% by 2014, and 4.5% by 2015.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Phased increase in Tier 1 capital requirements from 2013 to 2015.
subpolicies.control: Minimum requirements of 4.5% by 2013, 5.5% by 2014, and 6% by 2015.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Regulatory Adjustments to Common Equity Tier 1
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implementation of regulatory adjustments to Common Equity Tier 1 by 2018.
subpolicies.control: Deductions starting at 20% in 2014, increasing to 100% by 2018.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Phased exclusion of non-eligible capital from subsidiaries starting 2014.
subpolicies.control: 20% exclusion in 2014, increasing to 100% by 2018.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Grandfathering of Public Sector Capital Injections
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Existing public sector capital injections will be grandfathered until 2018.
subpolicies.control: No immediate changes until 1 January 2018.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Gradual phasing out of capital instruments that no longer qualify as Tier 1 or Tier 2.
subpolicies.control: Capped recognition starting at 90% in 2013, reducing by 10% each year.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Exclusion of Non-Qualifying Instruments from Common Equity Tier 1
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments not meeting criteria will be excluded from Common Equity Tier 1 as of 2013.
subpolicies.control: Phased out for specific instruments issued by non-joint stock companies.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reforms to capture all material risks in the capital framework effective January 2013.
subpolicies.control: Ensures comprehensive risk coverage for on- and off-balance sheet risks.

sections.title: Asset value correlation multiplier for large financial institutions

policies.policy_title: Asset Value Correlation Multiplier for Large Financial Institutions
policies.policy_description: A regulatory framework to address counterparty credit risk, credit valuation adjustments, and wrong-way risk for large financial institutions.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks with permission to use the internal models method (IMM) for calculating counterparty credit risk (CCR) regulatory capital.
policies.objective: To determine the default risk capital charge for counterparty credit risk using Effective EPE with stressed parameters.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must use the greater of the portfolio-level capital charge based on Effective EPE using current market data and the stress calibration.
subpolicies.control: The stress calibration must be consistent across the whole portfolio and not applied on a counterparty basis.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must employ current market data and at least three years of historical data to estimate parameters of the Effective EPE model.
subpolicies.control: Data must be updated quarterly or more frequently if market conditions warrant.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must demonstrate quarterly that the stress period coincides with increased credit spreads for a representative selection of counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Mapping of counterparties to specific credit spread data based on region, internal rating, and business types is required.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must create benchmark portfolios vulnerable to the same main risk factors and calculate exposures using stressed inputs.
subpolicies.control: Supervisors may adjust the stress calibration if benchmark portfolio exposures deviate substantially.

sections.title: Collateralised counterparties and margin period of risk

policies.policy_title: Collateralised Counterparties and Margin Period of Risk
policies.policy_description: Framework for the treatment of counterparty credit risk and capital charges associated with credit value adjustments (CVA) in OTC derivatives.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks with counterparty credit risk exposures.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital to cover the risk of mark-to-market losses on expected counterparty risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for calculating the CVA capital charge based on the bank's approved methods for counterparty credit risk.
subpolicies.control: Include transactions with central counterparties and securities financing transactions only if deemed material by the supervisor.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Advanced CVA Risk Capital Charge for Banks with IMM Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Calculation of the CVA capital charge using a VaR model that considers changes in counterparties' credit spreads.
subpolicies.control: Must use the specified formula for CVA calculation based on the bank's approved VaR model.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Short Cut Method for Margined Trades
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Application of the CVA risk capital charge for banks using the short cut method for collateralised OTC derivatives.
subpolicies.control: Assume a constant expected exposure profile based on the effective expected positive exposure.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Inclusion of non-IMM netting sets in the CVA risk capital charge for banks with IMM approval for the majority of their businesses.
subpolicies.control: Assume a constant expected exposure profile based on the EAD computed under CEM or SM.

policies.policy_title: Collateralised Counterparties and Margin Period of Risk
policies.policy_description: Regulations regarding the calculation of CVA risk capital charges for collateralised and uncollateralised OTC derivatives counterparties.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks calculating CVA risk capital charges.
policies.objective: To ensure appropriate risk assessment and capital charge calculation for CVA risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which the advanced CVA risk charge can be applied, including the requirement for supervisory approval.
subpolicies.control: Only exposures with supervisory approval for modelling specific risk of debt instruments are included.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Methodology for calculating the CVA risk capital charge, including components of general and specific credit spread risks.
subpolicies.control: CVA risk capital charge must be calculated as a standalone market risk charge.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for hedges that can be included in the CVA risk capital charge calculation.
subpolicies.control: Only specific types of hedges, such as single-name CDSs and index CDSs, are eligible.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Calculation method for banks without required approvals to use advanced CVA risk charge.
subpolicies.control: A portfolio capital charge must be calculated using a specified formula.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mapping of counterparty ratings to weights for the calculation of capital charges.
subpolicies.control: Weights are assigned based on external ratings of counterparties.

policies.policy_description: Policy regarding the capital charge for potential mark-to-market losses related to CVA risk.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks with IMM approval and market-risk internal-models approval.
policies.objective: To determine the total CCR capital charge based on various components.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Calculation of the IMM capital charge based on current and stressed parameter calibrations for EAD.
subpolicies.control: Higher of the IMM capital charge based on current or stressed parameter calibrations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Risk Weights for OTC Derivative Exposures
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Application of risk weights to OTC derivative exposures for IRB banks with full maturity adjustment.
subpolicies.control: Risk weights calculated with PD and M set equal to 1, contingent on demonstration to national supervisor.

policies.policy_description: Framework for calculating the total CCR capital charge for banks based on their approval status and risk exposure.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks operating with central counterparties under Basel III guidelines.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital against counterparty credit risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Capital Charge for Banks with IMM Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determination of total CCR capital charge for banks with Internal Models Method (IMM) approval and without Specific Risk VaR approval for bonds.
subpolicies.control: Total CCR capital charge is the sum of the higher IMM capital charge based on current or stressed parameter calibrations and the standardized CVA risk capital charge.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Capital Charge for All Other Banks
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determination of total CCR capital charge for banks not qualifying for IMM approval.
subpolicies.control: Total CCR capital charge is the sum of the CEM or SM based capital charge and the standardized CVA risk capital charge.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition and calculation of 'Outstanding EAD' for OTC derivative counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Outstanding EAD is defined as the greater of zero and the difference between the sum of EADs and the recognized CVA loss.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for banks to identify exposures that give rise to greater general wrong-way risk.
subpolicies.control: Stress testing and scenario analyses must be conducted to identify risk factors positively correlated with counterparty creditworthiness.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Policies for managing specific wrong-way risk associated with counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Banks must have procedures to identify, monitor, and control specific wrong-way risk, with differentiated EAD calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implementation of the asset value correlation (AVC) multiplier for large financial institutions.
subpolicies.control: Revisions to the calculation of risk-weighted assets based on PD and LGD measurements.

policies.policy_description: Defines the capital requirement for defaulted exposures based on loss given default (LGD) and expected loss.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions managing defaulted exposures.
policies.objective: To ensure sufficient capital is held against potential losses from defaulted exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Risk-weighted asset amount for defaulted exposure is calculated as the product of capital requirement (K), 12.5, and exposure at default (EAD).
subpolicies.control: Ensure accurate calculation of K and EAD.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A multiplier of 1.25 is applied to the correlation parameter for exposures to regulated financial institutions with total assets >= US $100 billion.
subpolicies.control: Verify asset size using the most recent audited financial statements.

policies.policy_title: Margin Period of Risk for Collateralised Counterparties
policies.policy_description: Establishes supervisory floors for margin periods of risk based on transaction types and conditions.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks engaging in collateralised transactions.
policies.objective: To mitigate risk associated with collateralised transactions through appropriate margin periods.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Imposes a supervisory floor of five business days for repo-style transactions and ten business days for other transactions.
subpolicies.control: Monitor transaction types and apply appropriate supervisory floors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Increased Supervisory Floor for High-Volume Trades
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Imposes a supervisory floor of 20 business days for netting sets with over 5,000 trades or involving illiquid collateral.
subpolicies.control: Track trade volumes and assess collateral liquidity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If more than two margin call disputes occur on a netting set over two quarters, the margin period of risk must be at least double the supervisory floor.
subpolicies.control: Document and review margin call disputes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines minimum holding periods for various transaction types based on daily re-margining.
subpolicies.control: Ensure compliance with minimum holding periods as specified.

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the method for modeling Expected Positive Exposure (EPE) for counterparties that utilize margin agreements.
policies.scope: Applicable to institutions that model EPE without margin agreements but require a method for margined counterparties.
policies.objective: To provide a standardized approach for modeling EPE in the context of margin agreements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the specific method to be used for modeling EPE for counterparties that have margin agreements.
subpolicies.control: Ensure compliance with Basel III revisions as published in December 2017.

sections.title: Enhanced counterparty credit risk management requirements

policies.policy_title: Enhanced Counterparty Credit Risk Management Requirements
policies.policy_description: Requirements for managing counterparty credit risk in accordance with Basel III framework.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks managing counterparty credit risk.
policies.objective: To ensure robust management of counterparty credit risk and compliance with Basel III standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Total CCR Capital Charge for Banks with IMM Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Calculation of total CCR capital charge for banks with Internal Models Method (IMM) approval without Specific Risk VaR approval for bonds.
subpolicies.control: Sum of higher IMM capital charge based on current or stressed parameter calibrations and standardized CVA risk capital charge.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Total CCR Capital Charge for Other Banks
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Calculation of total CCR capital charge for banks not qualifying for IMM approval.
subpolicies.control: Sum of CEM or SM based capital charge and standardized CVA risk capital charge.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition and calculation of outstanding Exposure at Default (EAD) for OTC derivative counterparties.
subpolicies.control: EAD defined as the greater of zero and the difference between total EADs and recognized CVA loss.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for identifying exposures that give rise to general wrong-way risk.
subpolicies.control: Stress testing and scenario analyses to monitor wrong-way risk by product, region, and industry.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Policies for managing specific wrong-way risk associated with counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Separate treatment of transactions with identified specific wrong-way risk in EAD calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implementation of the asset value correlation (AVC) multiplier for large financial institutions.
subpolicies.control: Revisions to risk-weighted asset calculations based on PD and LGD measurements.

policies.policy_title: Enhanced Counterparty Credit Risk Management Requirements
policies.policy_description: Requirements for managing counterparty credit risk, including capital requirements and risk-weighted assets calculations.
policies.scope: Applicable to regulated and unregulated financial institutions.
policies.objective: To ensure adequate capital and risk management practices for counterparty exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the formula for calculating capital requirements based on PD, LGD, and maturity adjustments.
Describes the calculation of risk-weighted assets based on capital requirements and exposure at default.
subpolicies.control: RWA = K x 12.5 x EAD

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Imposes a multiplier for the correlation parameter for exposures to financial institutions based on asset size.
Increases the margin period of risk for transactions subject to daily re-margining and mark-to-market valuation.
subpolicies.control: Supervisory floors of five, ten, or twenty business days based on transaction type and conditions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishes minimum holding periods for various transaction types.
subpolicies.control: Minimum holding periods vary from five to twenty business days based on transaction type.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognition of internal market risk models for repo-style and similar transactions.
subpolicies.control: Minimum holding period of 5-business days for repo-style transactions.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for managing counterparty credit risk with a focus on the shortcut method for margined counterparties.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks that model Exposure at Default (EPE) without margin agreements.
policies.objective: To provide a method for banks that lack the sophistication to model EPE with margin agreements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allows banks to use a simplified method for calculating EPE for margined counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Adherence to Basel III revisions published in December 2017.

sections.title: Addressing reliance on external credit ratings and minimising cliff effects

policies.policy_title: Addressing reliance on external credit ratings and minimising cliff effects
policies.policy_description: This policy addresses the reliance on external credit ratings and aims to minimize cliff effects in the context of effective exposure calculations.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using internal models for credit risk management.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate measurement of Effective EPE and improve operational performance in collateral management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the method for calculating Effective EPE for margined counterparties, including the use of collateral and exposure adjustments.
subpolicies.control: Backtesting to ensure consistency with shortcut method predictions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Explicitly disallows the capture of downgrade triggers in Exposure at Default (EAD) calculations.
subpolicies.control: Insertion of new paragraph 41(iv) into Annex 4.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishes requirements for a collateral management unit responsible for margin calls and dispute management.
subpolicies.control: Daily reporting of margin call metrics and operational performance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mandates regular independent reviews of the Counterparty Credit Risk (CCR) management system.
subpolicies.control: Annual reviews addressing documentation, organization, integration, and data integrity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires banks to manage liquidity risks associated with collateral reuse under adverse market conditions.
subpolicies.control: Policies must account for potential incoming margin calls and ensure timely collateral management.

policies.policy_title: Addressing reliance on external credit ratings and minimising cliff effects
policies.policy_description: Framework for transforming non-cash OTC collateral into cash-equivalent and implementing supervisory haircuts for non-cash OTC collateral.
policies.scope: Banks involved in OTC derivatives and securities-financing transactions.
policies.objective: To ensure robust collateral management and accurate EAD calculations for OTC derivatives and SFTs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Supervisory Haircuts for Non-Cash OTC Collateral
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must use haircuts that meet the standards of the financial collateral comprehensive method or standard supervisory haircuts for non-cash collateral in EAD calculations.
subpolicies.control: Incorporation of paragraph 61(i) in Annex 4.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must model non-cash collateral jointly with the exposure in EAD calculations for securities-financing transactions if the internal model includes the effect of collateral.
subpolicies.control: Incorporation of paragraph 61(ii) in Annex 4.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must devote sufficient resources to the orderly operation of margin agreements for OTC derivatives and SFT counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Incorporation of paragraph 115(i) in the main text.

sections.title: Standardised inferred rating treatment for long-term exposures

policies.policy_title: Standardised Inferred Rating Treatment for Long-Term Exposures
policies.policy_description: A framework for the treatment of long-term exposures under Basel III regulations, focusing on effective exposure calculations and collateral management.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using the internal models method for calculating Effective EPE.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate measurement of Effective EPE and improve operational performance in collateral management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the method for calculating Effective EPE for margined counterparties, including considerations for collateral and exposure changes.
subpolicies.control: Requires backtesting to ensure consistency with the shortcut method prediction and adjustments for underestimated effective EPE.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Prohibits the inclusion of downgrade triggers in the calculation of Exposure at Default (EAD) based on collateral agreements.
subpolicies.control: Mandates that banks using internal models must not capture reductions in EAD due to collateral agreements triggered by counterparty credit quality deterioration.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Operational Performance Improvement for Collateral Department
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishes requirements to enhance the operational efficiency of the collateral management department.
subpolicies.control: Incorporates new operational requirements into Annex 4 to improve collateral management practices.

sections.title: Incentive to avoid getting exposures rated

policies.policy_title: Incentive to avoid getting exposures rated
policies.policy_description: Framework for managing collateral and counterparty credit risk in accordance with Basel III regulations.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using the internal model method for collateral management.
policies.objective: To ensure effective management of collateral and counterparty credit risk, maintaining operational performance and compliance with regulatory standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Responsibilities of the collateral management unit including margin calls, data integrity, and reporting.
subpolicies.control: Daily calculation of margin calls, management of disputes, and reconciliation of data.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for internal reporting on collateral management to senior management.
subpolicies.control: Regular reporting on types of collateral, margin call disputes, and trends.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Independent Review of CCR Management System
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regular independent review of the counterparty credit risk management system.
subpolicies.control: Annual review addressing documentation, organization, risk integration, model approval, and data integrity.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for banks applying the internal model method regarding the establishment and operation of a collateral management unit.
policies.scope: Banks applying the internal model method under Basel III
policies.objective: To ensure effective management of collateral and margin calls.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The collateral management unit must control the integrity of data used for margin calls and ensure regular reconciliation with relevant data sources.
subpolicies.control: Data integrity and reconciliation processes

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The unit must track the reuse of collateral and the rights given to counterparties, including categories of reused collateral and terms of reuse.
subpolicies.control: Collateral tracking and reporting mechanisms

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Senior management must allocate sufficient resources and staff to the collateral management unit to ensure operational performance during normal and crisis conditions.
subpolicies.control: Resource allocation and staffing policies

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The collateral management unit must produce and maintain regular reports on collateral management information for senior management.
subpolicies.control: Internal reporting standards and frequency

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Independent Review of CCR Management System
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The bank must conduct regular independent reviews of the Counterparty Credit Risk (CCR) management system through internal audits.
subpolicies.control: Audit and review processes for CCR management

policies.policy_description: Framework for mitigating risks associated with collateral management and internal models in banking.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks employing internal models for risk management.
policies.objective: To ensure the accuracy and reliability of internal models and collateral management practices.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensure consistency, timeliness, and reliability of data sources used in internal models.
subpolicies.control: Independence of data sources and frequent backtesting of models.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Manage liquidity risks related to margin calls and collateral reuse.
subpolicies.control: Policies must account for adverse market shocks and ensure timely posting or returning of collateral.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Supervisory Haircuts for Non-Cash OTC Collateral
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implement supervisory haircuts when transforming non-cash collateral into cash-equivalent.
subpolicies.control: Use of haircuts that meet financial collateral comprehensive method standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Model non-cash collateral jointly with underlying securities for OTC derivatives and SFTs.
subpolicies.control: Include collateral effects in EAD calculations for securities-financing transactions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensure sufficient resources for the orderly operation of margin agreements.
subpolicies.control: Appropriate collateral management policies must be in place.

sections.title: Unsolicited ratings and recognition of ECAIs

policies.policy_description: Policies to control, monitor and report risks associated with margin agreements.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks managing collateral agreements.
policies.objective: To mitigate risks related to collateral management including volatility, concentration, reuse, and rights surrender.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Management of risks related to the volatility and liquidity of securities exchanged as collateral.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of collateral risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Management of concentration risks to particular types of collateral.
subpolicies.control: Reporting and monitoring of collateral concentration.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Management of risks associated with the reuse of collateral including potential liquidity shortfalls.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of collateral reuse practices.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Management of rights surrendered on collateral posted to counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Reporting on rights surrender.

policies.policy_description: Establishment of standard supervisory haircuts for securitisation collateral.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using securitisation collateral.
policies.objective: To ensure consistent application of haircuts for securitisation collateral across banks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Re-securitisations are not eligible financial collateral regardless of credit ratings.
subpolicies.control: Application of supervisory haircuts method.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implementation of standardised supervisory haircuts based on issue rating and residual maturity.
subpolicies.control: Daily mark-to-market and remargining practices.

policies.policy_description: Qualitative requirement for Probability of Default (PD) estimates for highly leveraged counterparties.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks assessing highly leveraged counterparties.
policies.objective: To ensure PD estimates reflect asset performance during stressed periods.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: PD estimates should reflect performance based on stressed periods.
subpolicies.control: Incorporation of stress testing in PD estimation.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory framework for managing capital conservation buffers in banks.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks under Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain a capital buffer to absorb losses during periods of financial stress.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Policies to control, monitor, and report risks associated with margin agreements and collateral management.
subpolicies.control: Includes monitoring volatility, liquidity, concentration risks, and reuse of collateral.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishes standard supervisory haircuts for various types of securitisation collateral.
subpolicies.control: Specifies haircuts based on issue rating and residual maturity for different securities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Qualitative requirements for PD estimates reflecting stressed performance of assets for highly leveraged counterparties.
subpolicies.control: PD estimates must consider stressed periods for asset performance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulatory capital treatment for exposures to central counterparties based on compliance with CPSS-IOSCO standards.
subpolicies.control: Standards to be finalized post-consultation and impact study.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Enhanced Counterparty Credit Risk Management Requirements
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Revisions to ensure robustness of banks' estimates of alpha in credit risk models.
subpolicies.control: Requires consistent computation, independent validation, and quarterly reviews.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Comprehensive stress testing program for counterparty credit risk, including various elements.
subpolicies.control: Includes trade capture, exposure aggregation, and multifactor stress testing scenarios.

policies.policy_description: Guidelines for portfolio monitoring, hedging, and management of concentrations in banks.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks under the capital conservation framework.
policies.objective: To enhance the resilience of banks by ensuring effective risk management practices.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Senior management must integrate stress testing into the risk management framework and culture.
subpolicies.control: Results of stress testing must be compared to risk appetite guidelines and discussed when risks are excessive.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks should conduct reverse stress tests to identify extreme but plausible adverse scenarios.
subpolicies.control: Identification of scenarios that could lead to significant adverse outcomes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: EPE models must include transaction-specific information and account for margining effects.
subpolicies.control: Models must consider margin agreements, frequency of margin calls, and thresholds of unmargined exposure.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing capital conservation in banking systems.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions under Basel III.
policies.objective: To ensure resilience in banks through effective collateral management and risk control.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Policies to control, monitor, and report risks associated with margin agreements and collateral.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of risk exposure, concentration risk, reuse of collateral, and rights surrender.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishment of standard supervisory haircuts for securitisation collateral.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of haircuts based on debt securities ratings and residual maturity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Qualitative requirements for PD estimates reflecting stressed asset performance.
subpolicies.control: Adjustment of PD estimates based on performance during stressed periods.

policies.policy_description: A global regulatory framework for more resilient banks and banking systems.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and banking systems globally.
policies.objective: To enhance the resilience of banks and banking systems through improved risk management and regulatory capital requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulatory capital treatment for exposures to central counterparties based on compliance with CPSS-IOSCO standards.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with enhanced CPSS-IOSCO standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for banks to ensure robust estimates of alpha and assess model risk.
subpolicies.control: Quarterly review of estimates and independent validation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Comprehensive stress testing program for counterparty credit risk including trade capture and exposure aggregation.
subpolicies.control: Integration of stress test results into regular reporting to senior management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for EPE models to include transaction-specific information and account for margining effects.
subpolicies.control: Model must adhere to the framework's rules for collateral.

policies.policy_description: Framework for the recognition of credit protection and the operation of capital conservation buffers.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and banking systems globally
policies.objective: To ensure resilience in banks through proper capital management and risk assessment.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which credit protection can be recognized for securitisation exposures, including requirements for external ratings.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with paragraphs 189 to 194 of Basel III.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must consistently use chosen ECAIs ratings for risk weighting and management, with specific rules regarding solicited and unsolicited ratings.
subpolicies.control: Supervisory authority approval for using unsolicited ratings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for maintaining capital buffers outside periods of stress to ensure banks can absorb losses.
subpolicies.control: Banks should hold capital above regulatory minimum and manage discretionary distributions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recommendations for banks to rebuild capital buffers after drawdowns, including reducing discretionary earnings distributions.
subpolicies.control: Consideration of raising new capital from private sector as an alternative.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory framework for managing counterparty credit risk through qualitative criteria and model validation.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using models for counterparty credit risk management.
policies.objective: To ensure banks have sound counterparty credit risk management systems and to set standards for model validation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must conduct regular backtesting to compare risk measures generated by models against realized risk measures.
subpolicies.control: Regular program of backtesting must be established.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Initial validation and ongoing periodic review of the IMM model must be independent of model developers.
subpolicies.control: Documented validation process must be in place.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board of directors and senior management must be actively involved in risk control processes.
subpolicies.control: Daily reports must be reviewed by senior management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The internal risk measurement model must be integrated into daily risk management processes.
subpolicies.control: Risk measurement outputs must inform planning and monitoring.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must ensure compliance with documented internal policies regarding the risk measurement system.
subpolicies.control: Routine checks for compliance must be established.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Independent Review of Risk Measurement System
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regular independent reviews of the risk measurement system must be conducted.
subpolicies.control: Reviews should address various aspects of the risk management process.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must document the initial and ongoing validation processes of their IMM model.
subpolicies.control: Documentation must allow third-party recreation of analysis.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must define criteria for assessing their EPE models and document processes for addressing unacceptable performance.
subpolicies.control: Written policy must be in place.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must define how representative counterparty portfolios are constructed for EPE model validation.
subpolicies.control: Clear guidelines for portfolio construction must be established.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Validation must assess multiple statistics of model distributions for EPE models.
subpolicies.control: Comprehensive validation processes must be implemented.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must carry out backtesting using historical data on market risk factors prior to supervisory approval.
subpolicies.control: Backtesting must cover various time horizons and market conditions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Pricing models used for counterparty credit risk exposure must be tested as part of model validation.
subpolicies.control: Models must account for nonlinearity and other relevant factors.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing counterparty credit risk through effective exposure modeling and validation.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using Internal Models Method (IMM) for counterparty credit risk.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate measurement and management of counterparty credit risk exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must verify that transactions are assigned to the appropriate netting set within the model.
subpolicies.control: Regular verification and validation of transaction assignments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Static, historical backtesting on representative counterparty portfolios must be conducted at regular intervals.
subpolicies.control: Backtesting must assess sensitivity to material risk factors and correlations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: EPE models and risk measures must undergo validation and performance assessment to identify poor performance.
subpolicies.control: Ongoing validation and assessment of model performance across all counterparties.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: An independent unit responsible for the design and implementation of the counterparty credit risk management system.
