subpolicies.control: Daily reporting and analysis of risk measurement model outputs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Address reliance on external credit ratings and minimize cliff effects in risk weight assessments.
subpolicies.control: Standardized inferred rating treatment for long-term exposures.

policies.policy_title: Basel III Compliance for Counterparty Credit Risk Management
policies.policy_description: Establishes the requirements for banks to manage counterparty credit risk using models.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks using models for counterparty credit risk management.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks have sound and effectively implemented counterparty credit risk management systems.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must conduct a regular program of backtesting to compare risk measures generated by the model against realized risk measures.
subpolicies.control: Ex-post comparison of risk measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must carry out initial validation and ongoing periodic review of their IMM model, independent of model developers.
subpolicies.control: Validation and review must be independent.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The board of directors and senior management must be actively involved in the risk control process.
subpolicies.control: Daily reports from the independent risk control unit must be reviewed by senior management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Integration of Risk Measurement into Daily Management
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The bank's internal risk measurement exposure model must be integrated into day-to-day risk management processes.
subpolicies.control: Output of the model must be part of planning and monitoring.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Use of Risk Measurement System with Exposure Limits
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The risk measurement system should be used alongside internal trading and exposure limits.
subpolicies.control: Exposure limits must relate to the risk measurement model consistently.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Compliance with Internal Policies and Procedures
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks should ensure compliance with documented internal policies regarding the risk measurement system.
subpolicies.control: Risk management manual must describe principles and empirical techniques.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Independent Review of Risk Measurement System
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: An independent review of the risk measurement system must be conducted regularly as part of internal auditing.
subpolicies.control: Regular independent reviews must be part of the auditing process.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for the management and validation of counterparty credit risk models and overall risk management processes in banks.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and banking systems under Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure resilience in banks through effective risk management and validation processes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regular review of the overall risk management process, including documentation adequacy and risk control unit organization.
subpolicies.control: Review should occur at least annually.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Integration of counterparty credit risk measures into daily risk management practices.
subpolicies.control: Approval process for counterparty credit risk models must be established.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Validation of significant changes in the risk measurement process and ongoing validation of counterparty credit risk models.
subpolicies.control: Periodic reviews by management with sufficient authority.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must document the initial and ongoing validation process of their IMM model and risk measures.
subpolicies.control: Documentation must allow third-party recreation of analysis.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of criteria for assessing EPE models and written policy for addressing unacceptable performance.
subpolicies.control: Written policy must describe the remediation process.

policies.policy_description: A framework to manage countercyclical risks specific to the bank's operations.
policies.scope: Applicable to all business trading units and independent risk control units within the bank.
policies.objective: To ensure a robust risk management process that integrates counterparty credit risk measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regular review of the overall risk management process, addressing documentation adequacy, organization of risk control units, and integration of counterparty credit risk measures.
subpolicies.control: Review must occur at least annually.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Approval process for counterparty credit risk models used by front office and back office personnel.
subpolicies.control: Models must be validated and approved by appropriate management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Validation of significant changes in the risk measurement process and ongoing validation of counterparty credit risk models.
subpolicies.control: Must include backtesting and periodic review by management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must document the process for initial and ongoing validation of their IMM model and risk measures.
subpolicies.control: Documentation must enable third-party recreation of analysis and risk measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of criteria to assess EPE models and documentation of the process for addressing unacceptable performance.
subpolicies.control: Written policy must be in place for remediation.

sections.title: Extension of the capital conservation buffer

policies.policy_title: Extension of the capital conservation buffer
policies.policy_description: Regulatory requirements for banks regarding the validation of Expected Positive Exposure (EPE) models and risk measures.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks using EPE models for counterparty credit risk.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate validation and performance assessment of EPE models and risk measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must conduct backtesting using historical data on market risk factors prior to supervisory approval, considering various prediction time horizons.
subpolicies.control: Backtesting must cover a range of market conditions and include long time horizons of at least 1 year.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Pricing models used to calculate counterparty credit risk exposure must be tested as part of the model validation process.
subpolicies.control: Models for options must account for nonlinearity with respect to market risk factors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: EPE models must capture transaction-specific information to aggregate exposures at the netting set level.
subpolicies.control: Banks must verify correct assignment of transactions to netting sets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regular static historical backtesting on representative counterparty portfolios must be conducted.
subpolicies.control: Portfolios must be chosen based on sensitivity to material risk factors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The performance of EPE models and risk measures must be subject to good backtesting practices.
subpolicies.control: Backtesting programs must identify poor performance in risk measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Significant differences between realized exposures and forecast distributions may indicate model issues.
subpolicies.control: Supervisors may require additional capital to be held while problems are resolved.

policies.policy_description: Establishes guidelines for the frequency of calculation and disclosure of counterparty exposure and risk measures.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using internal modelling methods for calculating exposure.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate and timely validation of EPE models and risk measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must validate their EPE models and relevant risk measures out to time horizons commensurate with the maturity of trades.
subpolicies.control: Regular testing against independent benchmarks as part of the ongoing validation process.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ongoing validation includes an assessment of recent performance and the frequency of parameter updates.
subpolicies.control: Regular assessments covering all counterparties for which models are used.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A more conservative measure than the metric used to calculate regulatory EAD may be used with prior supervisory approval.
subpolicies.control: Regular validation of conservatism and assessment during supervisory reviews.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: An independent unit responsible for the design and implementation of counterparty credit risk management.
subpolicies.control: Produces daily reports on risk measurement model outputs and evaluates relationships between credit exposure and trading limits.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Modifications to risk weight assessments based on specific assessments for issued debts.
subpolicies.control: General principles apply for claims not invested in specific assessed issues.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory framework for recognizing credit protection provided to securitisation exposures.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using the Foundation IRB approach.
policies.objective: To ensure proper recognition of credit protection under Basel III.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the range of eligible guarantors for credit protection recognition.
subpolicies.control: Requirements outlined in paragraphs 189 to 194 must be met.

policies.policy_description: Guidelines on the consistent use of External Credit Assessment Institutions (ECAIs) ratings.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks for risk weighting and risk management.
policies.objective: To prevent arbitrary changes in the use of ECAIs ratings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines on the use of solicited and unsolicited ratings from ECAIs.
subpolicies.control: National supervisory authorities may allow the use of unsolicited ratings if quality is not inferior.

policies.policy_description: Operational guidelines for the capital conservation buffer to ensure banks maintain capital outside periods of stress.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks.
policies.objective: To ensure banks build up capital buffers to absorb losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recommendations for banks to hold capital buffers above regulatory minimums.
subpolicies.control: Banks should reduce discretionary distributions of earnings to rebuild capital buffers.

policies.policy_description: Framework for assessing leverage ratios in banking systems under Basel III.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions under Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure banks assess credit risk accurately and maintain adequate capital adequacy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Senior unsecured claims benefit from high quality issuer assessments, while unassessed claims are treated as unrated.
subpolicies.control: Claims ranking and risk weight assignment based on issuer assessments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must have methodologies to assess credit risk for individual borrowers and portfolios.
subpolicies.control: Evaluation of risk weights applied to exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for determining eligibility of external credit assessment institutions (ECAIs).
subpolicies.control: Assessment of objectivity, independence, transparency, disclosure, resources, and credibility.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operational criteria for using external credit assessments in standardized and IRB approaches.
subpolicies.control: Public availability of assessments and methodologies.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recognition of credit protection from eligible entities for risk mitigation.
subpolicies.control: Criteria for recognizing credit protection based on risk weight.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must use chosen ECAIs consistently and may not cherry-pick ratings.
subpolicies.control: Regulation on the use of solicited and unsolicited ratings.

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the operation of the capital conservation buffer designed to ensure that banks build up capital buffers outside periods of stress.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain capital buffers to absorb losses during periods of stress.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks should hold buffers of capital above the regulatory minimum outside of periods of stress.
subpolicies.control: Banks must monitor and maintain capital buffers as per regulatory requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: When buffers have been drawn down, banks should look to rebuild them by reducing discretionary distributions of earnings.
subpolicies.control: This includes reducing dividend payments, share-backs, and staff bonus payments, or raising new capital from the private sector.

policies.policy_description: A global regulatory framework for more resilient banks and banking systems.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions.
policies.objective: To enhance the resilience of banks and banking systems through improved risk assessment and management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Only senior claims on an issuer with a high-quality assessment will benefit from that assessment; other unassessed claims will be treated as unrated.
subpolicies.control: Claims ranking and risk weight assignment.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must have methodologies to assess credit risk for individual borrowers and at the portfolio level, including unrated exposures.
subpolicies.control: Evaluation of overall capital adequacy based on inherent risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: National supervisors must continuously determine if an external credit assessment institution meets eligibility criteria based on IOSCO's Code of Conduct.
subpolicies.control: Public supervisory process for ECAI recognition.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: ECAIs must meet six criteria including objectivity, independence, transparency, and disclosure of methodologies.
subpolicies.control: Public availability of assessment methodologies and results.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Operational Requirements for External Credit Assessments
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: External credit assessments must come from eligible ECAIs and be publicly available free of charge.
subpolicies.control: Accessibility of ratings and related analyses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recognition of credit protection from specified entities with lower risk weights than the counterparty.
subpolicies.control: Criteria for eligible guarantors and protection providers.

sections.title: Definition and calculation of the leverage ratio

policies.policy_description: Defines the leverage ratio and outlines the methodology for its calculation in accordance with Basel III regulations.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions under Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure banks assess credit risk accurately and maintain adequate capital adequacy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details on how issuer assessments apply to senior unsecured claims and the treatment of unassessed claims.
subpolicies.control: Claims are treated based on the quality of issuer assessments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks should have methodologies to assess credit risk for individual borrowers and portfolios.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of exposures must consider inherent risks and appropriate risk weights.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: National supervisors determine the eligibility of external credit assessment institutions based on IOSCO criteria.
subpolicies.control: ECAI assessments must be publicly available and transparent.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria that ECAIs must satisfy to be recognized, including objectivity, independence, and transparency.
subpolicies.control: ECAIs must disclose their methodologies and assessment processes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operational criteria for using external credit assessments in standardized and IRB approaches.
subpolicies.control: Assessments must be publicly available and free of charge.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recognition of credit protection from various entities and the conditions under which it is accepted.
subpolicies.control: Credit protection must come from entities with a lower risk weight than the counterparty.

policies.policy_description: Framework for assessing capital adequacy in relation to credit risk and exposure ratings.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions under Basel III guidelines.
policies.objective: To ensure banks have methodologies to assess credit risk and maintain adequate capital based on exposure ratings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Senior unsecured claims benefit from high quality issuer assessments, while other unassessed claims are treated as unrated.
subpolicies.control: Claims are assigned risk weights based on issuer assessments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must assess credit risk for both rated and unrated exposures and ensure risk weights are appropriate.
subpolicies.control: Evaluation of overall capital adequacy must consider inherent risks of exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: National supervisors determine ECAI eligibility based on IOSCO Code of Conduct Fundamentals.
subpolicies.control: Supervisory processes for ECAI recognition must be public to prevent barriers to entry.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: ECAIs must meet six criteria including objectivity, independence, and transparency.
subpolicies.control: Public availability of assessments and key elements of the assessment process.

policies.policy_description: ECAIs must disclose specific information regarding their assessments and methodologies.
policies.scope: Applicable to all ECAIs recognized by banks' national supervisors.
policies.objective: To ensure transparency and credibility in credit assessments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: ECAIs must disclose their code of conduct.
subpolicies.control: Public availability of the code of conduct.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: ECAIs must disclose the nature of their compensation arrangements with assessed entities.
subpolicies.control: Public availability of compensation arrangements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: ECAIs must disclose their assessment methodologies, including definitions and actual default rates.
subpolicies.control: Public availability of methodologies and default rates.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: ECAIs must disclose the transitions of assessments over time.
subpolicies.control: Public availability of transition data.

policies.policy_title: Operational Requirements for External Credit Assessments
policies.policy_description: Operational criteria for the use of external credit assessments in the securitisation framework.
policies.scope: Standardised and IRB approaches of the securitisation framework.
policies.objective: To ensure the use of credible and publicly available credit assessments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Only assessments from eligible ECAIs recognized by national supervisors are acceptable.
subpolicies.control: Public availability of assessments and methodologies.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ratings must be published in an accessible form and included in the ECAI’s transition matrix.
subpolicies.control: Free public access to ratings and analysis.

policies.policy_description: Recognition of credit protection from various entities.
policies.scope: Applicable to credit protection provided by eligible entities.
policies.objective: To define the range of eligible guarantors and protection providers.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recognition of credit protection from sovereign entities, PSEs, banks, and securities firms.
subpolicies.control: Lower risk weight than the counterparty.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Credit protection from externally rated entities is recognized, except for securitisation exposures.
subpolicies.control: Lower risk weight than the obligor.

policies.policy_description: Framework for credit protection and recognition of ratings in banking systems.
policies.scope: Banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To establish guidelines for credit protection and capital conservation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which credit protection can be recognized for securitisation exposures.
subpolicies.control: Entities must be rated BBB- or better and meet specific requirements outlined in paragraphs 189 to 194.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for banks on the use of solicited and unsolicited ratings from eligible credit assessment institutions.
subpolicies.control: Banks must use ratings consistently and may be allowed to use unsolicited ratings if deemed of equal quality.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for banks to maintain capital buffers outside periods of stress.
subpolicies.control: Banks should hold capital above regulatory minimum and reduce discretionary distributions to rebuild buffers.

sections.title: Annex 1: Calibration of the capital framework

policies.policy_description: A framework to ensure banks retain sufficient capital buffers during economic downturns.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks under the Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To strengthen the resilience of banks by limiting capital distributions when capital levels are low.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishes a capital conservation buffer of 2.5% above the minimum capital requirement.
subpolicies.control: Banks must retain a greater proportion of earnings as capital levels approach minimum requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Imposes constraints on capital distributions when banks' capital levels fall within the conservation range.
subpolicies.control: Distributions are limited as capital levels approach the minimum requirements, ensuring banks conserve earnings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must increase the share of earnings retained to rebuild capital buffers as they deplete.
subpolicies.control: Future predictions of recovery cannot justify generous distributions to stakeholders.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Aims to reduce procyclicality by ensuring capital remains available during periods of stress.
subpolicies.control: Encourages banks to conserve earnings rather than increase distributions during downturns.

sections.title: Annex 2: The 15% of common equity limit on specified items

policies.policy_title: 15% Common Equity Limit on Specified Items
policies.policy_description: Regulations governing the distribution of earnings based on Common Equity Tier 1 capital ratios.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks under Basel III framework
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital reserves and limit distributions to shareholders based on capital ratios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must conserve a percentage of earnings based on their CET1 capital ratio.
subpolicies.control: Earnings conservation requirements based on CET1 capital ratios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines what constitutes distributions and identifies exceptions.
subpolicies.control: Restrictions on dividends, share buybacks, and discretionary payments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Earnings are defined as distributable profits calculated before deductions for restricted distributions.
subpolicies.control: Calculation of earnings for distribution purposes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Application at Solo or Consolidated Level
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Framework should be applied at the consolidated level, with options for solo application by national supervisors.
subpolicies.control: Application scope of the capital conservation framework.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Supervisors can impose time limits on banks operating within the buffer range to prevent competitive exploitation.
subpolicies.control: Supervisory oversight on capital buffer usage.

policies.policy_description: The capital conservation buffer will be phased in to ensure banks rebuild capital buffers over time.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks under Basel III framework
policies.objective: To maintain a capital conservation buffer of 2.5% of RWAs by January 2019.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The capital conservation buffer will be phased in from 1 January 2016 to 1 January 2019, starting at 0.625% and increasing annually.
subpolicies.control: National authorities may impose shorter transition periods.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks below the 7% Common Equity Tier 1 target must maintain prudent earnings retention to meet the conservation buffer.
subpolicies.control: Focus on timely compliance with the conservation buffer.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The buffer will be divided into quartiles starting 1 January 2016, expanding as the capital conservation buffer is phased in.
subpolicies.control: Consideration of any countercyclical buffer in effect.

policies.policy_description: The countercyclical buffer aims to ensure that banking sector capital requirements account for macro-financial risks.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks experiencing excess credit growth.
policies.objective: To protect the banking system against potential losses during periods of excess credit growth.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: National authorities will monitor credit growth and assess whether it leads to system-wide risk.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of countercyclical buffer requirements when necessary.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Internationally active banks will calculate their countercyclical capital buffer based on credit exposures across jurisdictions.
subpolicies.control: Weighted average of buffer requirements in jurisdictions with credit exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Each jurisdiction will identify an authority to decide on the countercyclical capital buffer size.
subpolicies.control: Buffer can vary between 0% and 2.5% of risk-weighted assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Jurisdictions will pre-announce increases in the countercyclical buffer by up to 12 months.
subpolicies.control: Immediate effect for decreases in buffer levels.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks will have a countercyclical buffer of 0% to 2.5% based on their credit exposure portfolio.
subpolicies.control: Must meet buffer with Common Equity Tier 1 or other fully loss-absorbing capital.

policies.policy_description: Guidelines for allocating the bank's total credit risk charge related to private sector credit exposures across jurisdictions.
policies.scope: Applicable to all jurisdictions where the bank operates.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate allocation of credit risk charges to geographic locations of counterparties.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must collaborate with supervisors to translate specific risk charges into individual instrument risk weights allocated to geographic locations.
subpolicies.control: Requires supervisory approval and methodology for allocation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: In cases where charges cannot be broken down by individual instruments, allocate the charge based on the proportion of total exposure at default from each geographic region.
subpolicies.control: Calculation based on portfolio's total exposure at default.

policies.policy_description: Implementation of the countercyclical buffer through an extension of the capital conservation buffer.
policies.scope: Applies to banks with private sector credit exposures.
policies.objective: To maintain adequate capital levels during varying economic conditions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishes minimum capital conservation ratios at various levels of Common Equity Tier 1 capital ratio.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with specified capital ratios as outlined in the accompanying table.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires that the countercyclical buffer be met with Common Equity Tier 1 capital only until further guidance is issued.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of capital levels in relation to countercyclical buffer requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks should use an ultimate risk basis for assessing the jurisdiction of private sector credit exposures.
subpolicies.control: Assessment based on the location of the guarantor rather than the booking location.

policies.policy_description: Minimum capital conservation standards for banks based on Common Equity Tier 1 capital ratios.
policies.scope: Individual banks
policies.objective: To set minimum capital conservation ratios based on capital levels.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ratios expressed as a percentage of earnings based on quartiles of buffer.
subpolicies.control: 100% within first quartile, 80% within second quartile, 60% within third quartile, 40% within fourth quartile, 0% above top of buffer.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum capital conservation ratios based on levels of Common Equity Tier 1 capital subject to a 2.5% countercyclical buffer.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for banks to calculate and disclose countercyclical buffer requirements.
policies.scope: Banks
policies.objective: To ensure transparency and consistency in the calculation and disclosure of buffer requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Countercyclical buffer requirements must be calculated and disclosed with at least the same frequency as minimum capital requirements.
subpolicies.control: Based on latest jurisdictional countercyclical buffers.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must disclose the geographic breakdown of private sector credit exposures used in buffer calculations.
subpolicies.control: Included in public disclosures.

policies.policy_description: Phased implementation of the countercyclical buffer regime.
policies.scope: Countries with excessive credit growth
policies.objective: To gradually implement the countercyclical buffer alongside the capital conservation buffer.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Countercyclical buffer regime phased-in from January 2016 to January 2019.
subpolicies.control: Starts at 0.625% of RWAs and increases by 0.625 percentage points each year.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Countries experiencing excessive credit growth may accelerate the buildup of capital buffers.
subpolicies.control: Jurisdictions may implement larger countercyclical buffer requirements.

policies.policy_description: Introduction of a non-risk based leverage ratio as a supplementary measure to risk-based capital requirements.
policies.scope: Banks
policies.objective: To constrain excessive leverage and reinforce risk-based requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition and calculation of the leverage ratio based on capital and total exposure measures.
subpolicies.control: Minimum Tier 1 leverage ratio of 3% during the parallel run period.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Capital measure based on the new definition of Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Consistent measurement of capital and exposure to avoid double counting.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Exposure measure should generally follow the accounting measure of exposure.
subpolicies.control: Includes on-balance sheet items, SFT, and derivatives.

policies.policy_description: Outlines the transition period and monitoring for the leverage ratio implementation.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks under the Basel II and III Framework.
policies.objective: To assess the appropriateness of the minimum Tier 1 leverage ratio of 3% over a full credit cycle.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must calculate off-balance sheet items for leverage ratio using a uniform 100% credit conversion factor.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring by the Committee to ensure compliance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks should apply a 10% credit conversion factor for commitments that are unconditionally cancellable.
subpolicies.control: Further review by the Committee to ensure conservativeness.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Commences on 1 January 2011 to track components of the leverage ratio.
subpolicies.control: Development of templates for consistent tracking.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Runs from 1 January 2013 to 1 January 2017 to track leverage ratio and its components.
subpolicies.control: Banks required to calculate leverage ratio based on specified definitions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Bank level disclosure of the leverage ratio and its components starts on 1 January 2015.
subpolicies.control: Development of a disclosure template by the Committee.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Final adjustments to the definition and calibration of the leverage ratio based on the parallel run results.
subpolicies.control: Migration to Pillar 1 treatment on 1 January 2018.

policies.policy_description: Framework for the gradual implementation of capital requirements and ratios as per Basel III revisions.
policies.scope: Applicable to banking groups and their capital requirements.
policies.objective: To ensure banks meet minimum capital requirements while transitioning to new standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Gradual increase in the minimum common equity capital ratio from 3.5% to 7.0% over specified years.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring and compliance with phased capital ratio increases.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Incremental increase in the capital conservation buffer from 0.625% to 2.50% over specified years.
subpolicies.control: Ensuring banks maintain additional capital to absorb losses during periods of financial stress.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Gradual implementation of deductions from Common Equity Tier 1 capital, reaching 100% by 2018.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring the impact of deferred tax assets and other deductions on CET1.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishment of minimum Tier 1 capital requirements, increasing from 4.5% to 6.0%.
subpolicies.control: Compliance checks to ensure banks meet minimum Tier 1 capital ratios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Maintenance of a minimum total capital ratio of 8.0% throughout the transition period.
subpolicies.control: Regular assessments to ensure total capital adequacy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduction of a minimum liquidity coverage ratio standard, with an observation period.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring liquidity positions to ensure banks can meet short-term obligations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implementation of a minimum net stable funding ratio standard, with an observation period.
subpolicies.control: Ensuring banks maintain stable funding over a one-year horizon.
